Title: Decision No. 2629/1997/BYT-QD issuing the list of food commodities subject to the 1998 quality registration.
Keywords: agricultural commodities; registration; milk/dairy products; oils/oilseeds/fats; coffee/cocoa/tea; condiments/herbs/spices; meat; poultry products; sugar; cereals/grains/rice; vegetables/legumes; fruits/edible nuts; beverages; viticulture/oenological practices; mineral water; treated food
Abstract: The Decision contains a list of food commodities subject to the 1998 quality registration. Organizations and individuals of all economic sectors engaged in the production, processing and/or import of food commodities specified in the list shall have to make goods quality registration as prescribed in the Decision. The Department of Preventive Medicine shall control the implementation of the Decision. With particular reference to the list of aquatic food commodities subject to the 1998 quality registration, Decision No. 1606/QD-TDC of 21 October 1997 is to be applied (art. 1).

Title: Decision No. 980/1997/QD-TTg approving the salt production plan for the years 2000-2010.
Keywords: agricultural development; subsidy/incentive; condiments/herbs/spices
Abstract: Pursuant to the Law on the Organization of the Government of 1992, this Decision concerns the approval of the plan for salt production. The plan aims at the achievement of the following objectives: (a) to ensure sufficient supply of salt for daily consumption and exportation; (b) to promote the development of salt industry; (c) to expand the existing salt fields; (d) to allocate funds from the State budget; (e) to organize the salt production and distribution system under the State Salt Corporation according to the provisions of article 2.

Title: Ordinance on the Protection of Consumer Interests.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; consumer protection; fraud; internal trade
Abstract: This Ordinance sets out rights and responsibilities of consumers and the responsibilities of organizations and individuals in the production of goods and services, and provides for state supervision in relation with protection of interests of consumers. The last part of the act concerns the handling of complaints and offences in relation with consumer' interests. (30 articles)

Title: Regulation on the labeling of goods to be circulated in the country and of export as well as import goods.
Keywords: food safety and quality; agricultural commodities; beverages; packaging/labelling
Abstract: This Regulation prescribes rules for the labelling of goods manufactured in Vietnam as well as imported goods. Goods here includes raw and processed foodstuffs, and essential commodities. Food and drinks that are ready-packed and are to be consumed within 24 hours shall not be governed by the present Regulations. Chapter II contains provisions relative to the description of contents of goods, their duration, composition, etc. Chapter III concerns state control of labelling, whereas Chapter IV prescribes offences and penalties. (19 articles)

Title: Regulation on the State Control of import-export goods quality control.
Keywords: food safety and quality; international trade; inspection; standards
Abstract: This Regulation prescribes methods and procedures of State control of the quality of goods imported in or exported from Vietnam as well as the powers of authorities involved. The Regulation shall apply to goods specified in the List of Goods subject to State Quality Control issued by the Ministry of Science, Technology and the Environment. For goods controlled there shall be issued a certificate of the controlling agency pursuant to section 1.5. Goods that are already conform a Vietnamese Standard or a recognised foreign standard shall be exempt from control. Powers and responsibilities of State agencies involved in control are specified in Part 2. Part 3 contains rules relative to procedures and methods of control.

Title: Decision No. 228/1999/QD-TTg adding the objective of ensuring foodstuff quality, hygiene and safety to the National Target Program for Elimination of a Number of Social Diseases and Dangerous Epedemics.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; policy/planning; toxicity/poisoning
Abstract: This Decision places food hygiene and quality on the list of targets for the elimination of diseases affecting the population. The objective is to control food contamination and supervise food quality. The Ministry of Health shall direct the Steering Committee of the national Target program for the Elimination of a Number of Social Diseases and Dangerous Epidemics to select key projects in accordance with selected targets.

Title: Circular No. 34/1999/TT-BTM guiding the implementation of the Prime Minister's Decision No. 178/1999/QD-TTg issuing the Regulation on the labeling of goods to be circulated in the country and of export as well as import goods.
Keywords: food safety and quality; packaging/labelling
Abstract: This Circular further clarifies and complements the Regulation prescribes rules for the labelling of goods manufactured in Vietnam as well as imported goods. Goods here includes raw and processed foodstuffs, and essential commodities. The Circular prescribes rule relative to the description of contents of goods in labels, including indications of appellation, name and address of trader, quantity, ingredients, origin, and quality indication criteria. As far as the last indication is concerned, merchants shall describe on the label the technical parameters and quantitative indicators of the major quality criteria.

Title: Ordinance on goods quality control (No. 18/1999/PL-UBTVQH10).
Keywords: food safety and quality; inspection; certification; fraud
Abstract: This Ordinance makes provision for the control of the quality of goods in general, quality certification, quality standards, and quality control systems, and inspection of quality of goods. The Government shall promulgate the Vietnamese Standard on goods quality under Chapter II. Food shall be compulsorily conform to prescribed standards (sect. 12). Chapter II concerns certification of quality and quality control systems. Chapter IV sets out the responsibilities of producers and traders in relation with the quality of goods. Chapters V and VI provide for the supervision and control by the State of quality of goods, including inspection and examination of goods. Remaining provisions prescribe offences and penalties.

Title: Decision No. 244/1998/QD-TTg issuing the Regulation on operation of the Management Board of the National Target Program for Hunger Eradication and Poverty Alleviation.
Keywords: food security; institution; governance
Abstract: This Regulation lays down the principles of the Management Board of the National Target Program for Hunger Eradication and Povert Alleviation and defines the tasks assigned to it. The Board was established by Decision No. 80/1998/QD-TTg of 9 April, 1998.

Title: Decision No. 03/1999/QD-CDTQG promulgating the Regulation on bidding purchase and sale of national food reserve.
Keywords: food security
Abstract: This Decision of the National Reserves Department concerns regulation of bidding for the purchase of national food reserves to be organized by sub-departments of the National Reserves Department. The sale shall take place pursuant to Decisions of the National Reserves Department under Circular 02/1999/TT-CDTQG of 12 January 1999.

Title: Decision No. 20/1999/QD-TTg on the establishment and promulgation of the operation regulation of the Steering Board for the Management of Rice Export and Fertilizer Import.
Keywords: cereals/grains/rice; agricultural commodities; international trade; fertilizers/nutrients; plant production
Abstract: This Decision of the Prime Minister established the Steering Board for the Management of Rice Export and Fertilizer Import. The Board shall be headed by the Vice Minister of Trade.

Title: Decision No. 18/1999/QD-BNN-TCCB to additionally assign the task of disinfecting to the Coffee Research and Quality Control Centre.
Keywords: food safety and quality; treated food; agricultural commodities; coffee/cocoa/tea; hygiene/sanitary procedures; beverages
Abstract: The task of providing disinfecting services for import and export coffee in warehouses, containers and land transport is assigned to the Coffee Research and Quality Control Centre. The Head of the Plant Protection Department shall provide professional guidance and grant a license to disinfect to the Centre. The Director of the Centre shall implement this Decision and register disinfecting operations. (3 articles)

Title: Directive No. 008/1999/CT-TTg on enhancing the foodstuff quality, hygiene and safe guarantee.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection
Abstract: This instruction of the Prime Minister seeks to put an end to the insufficient control of food quality and hygiene and to restore discipline in the food production and food catering services. Every year a month of action for foodstuff quality, hygiene and safety shall be organized to mobilise the population and authorities to take part in prevention and combating food poisoning and diseases caused by food. The Directive also prescribes tasks of the Presidents of People's Committees of provinces and centrally run cities in relation with information of food quality and hygiene, inspection, handling of violations, etc. Also tasks and responsibilities of Ministries and their branch offices are defined.

Title: Circular No. 33/1999/TT-BTC guiding the management and allocation of non-business funds for the National Program for Hunger Elimination and Poverty Alleviation.
Keywords: food security; policy/planning
Abstract: The Central budget shall annually allocate a fund necessary for achieving the targets of the Program. Funds from non-business sources shall be channelled through the Ministry of Finance to Ministries and agencies taking part in the Program and by Finance and Pricing Offices of the provinces and centrally-run cities under the authorization of the Ministry of Finance to performs tasks under the Program in the relevant areas.

Title: Decree No. 19/1999/ND-CP on the production and supply of iodised salt for human use.
Keywords: food safety and quality; condiments/herbs/spices; standards; authorization/permit
Abstract: This Decree regulates the production and placing on the market of edible salt, excluding non-iodised salt. Edible salt and salt used for preparation of food shall be mixed with iodine according to prescribed standards. The Ministry of Agriculture shall plan and coordinate slat production in accordance with article 5. Chapter II prescribes criteria for the production of edible salt including conditions that production establishment shall meet. Chapter III defines the competence to grant certificates for salt production and procedures for granting such certificates. Provisions of Chapter IV and V concern packing and labelling, control of quality and other inspections and the punishment of the specified offences. (24 articles)

Title: Decision No. 650/2000/QD-BTS promulgating the Regulation on the State Inspection and Certification of Aquatic Goods Quality.
Keywords: food safety and quality; inspection; certification
Abstract: This Regulation prescribes the mode, contents and procedures of inspection and certification of the quality of aquatic products, and, in relation to this, defines duties and powers of State inspection agencies. It applies to all aquatic products, whether intended for exportation or domestic consumption, and to imported products. Inspecting agencies are appointed by the Ministry of Science, Technology and Environment and the Ministry of Aquatic Resources. Products originating from specified establishment shall be exempt from inspection (art. 4). Products shall not be exported, imported or domestically consumed without a quality certificate or a quality inspection exemption (art. 5). Articles 6 to 8 define duties and powers of owners of products and inspecting agencies. Articles 9 to 13 define the contents and procedures of inspection and the notification of inspection results. Remaining provisions deal with charges and fees, settlement of complaints and handling of violations. (18 articles)

Title: Decision No. 649/2000/QD-BTS promulgating the Regulation on Inspection and Recognition of Aquatic Products Manufacturing and Business Establishments Meeting Food Hygiene and Safety Standards.
Keywords: fish products; hygiene/sanitary procedures; food safety and quality; inspection; standards
Abstract: Article 2 of the Decision excludes from the application of this Regulation certain smaller fishing vessels and establishments operating in the retail and non-food sector. The Regulation prescribes the conditions and procedures for the recognition of establishments that are involved in production and trade of aquatic products that meet food hygiene and safety requirements described in article 3. Article 4 provides for the organization of inspection (the Inspecting Bodies), whereas article 5 defines the forms of inspection and verification. Establishments shall register with the Inspecting Bodies in accordance with provisions of article 8. Articles 18 to 21 spell out duties of establishments and Inspecting Bodies. The Attachments list, inter alia, standards to be complied with. (27 articles)

Title: Circular No. 116/2000/TT-BTC guiding the Regime of Management and Use of Fees for Aquatic Product Quality, Safety and Hygiene Control.
Keywords: fish products; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures
Abstract: A regulation to provide for the collection of fees for the control of food safety and hygiene by state bodies of aquatic products and the expenditure of those fees. The main body of the regulation is divided into four parts: use of the collected fee amounts; the elaboration and execution of fee collection-spending estimates; regulation of fees for aquatic product quality, safety and hygiene control; final settlement of collection-spending of fees for aquatic product quality, safety and hygiene control. The Ministry of Aquatic Resources shall establish control mechanisms for correct collection of fee.

Title: Decision No. 21/2001/QD-TTg ratifying the National Strategy on Nutrition for the 2001-2010 Period.
Keywords: food security; policy/planning; nutrition
Abstract: The Strategy here ratified has as its overall objective to improve markedly nutrition in Viet Nam by the year 2010, with a priority of all mothers and children, to create more balance in food rations in various regions of the country and to improve those rations and to increase and meet hygienic standards. It is also a declared objective to fight nutrition-related health problems. Article 1 sets out also specific objective of the Strategy, indicating targets. Article 2 prescribes major solutions to nutrition problems and policies.

Title: Joint Circular No. 04/2001/TTLT/YT-TM-VHTT-UBBVCSTEVN guiding the implementation of Decree No. 74/2000/ND/CP of the Government on trading in and use of mother milk substitutes to protect and encourage breast-feeding.
Keywords: breastfeeding/infant food; food safety and quality; milk/dairy products; special dietary uses; nutrition
Abstract: This Circular was prepared jointly by the Ministries of Health, of Trade, of Culture and Information, the Prime Minister and the Vietnam Committee for Child Protection It consists of 7 Parts: general provisions (I); Information, education, communications (II); Advertisement (III); Trading in mother milk substitutes (IV); responsibility of the Obstetric and Paediatric departments and the medical cadres and personnel of these establishments (V); State management responsibilities (VI); implementation provisions (VII). Article 2 of Part I outlines the application sphere of the Circular. Before being placed on the market, all mother milk substitutes must have their food quality, hygiene and safety characteristics registered with the Ministry of Health.

Title: Circular No. 04/2001/TT-BTM guiding the implementation of the Prime Minister's Directive No. 28/2000/CT-TTg on the implementation of the Regulation on Goods Labelling, issued together with Decision No. 178/19999/QD-TTg of 3 August 1999.
Keywords: food safety and quality; packaging/labelling
Abstract: The Ministry of Trade issues this Circular to ensure the  serious and effective  implementation of the Regulation on the labelling of goods of 1999. State management bodies shall intensify the inspection and control of the implementation of the Regulation regarding the labelling of goods placed upon the market and the handling of violations thereof in accordance with directions laid down in the present Circular. The Directions prescribe form and content of inspection of the labelling of goods.

Title: Provisional Regulation on the announcement of quality standards of specialized aquatic goods.
Keywords: fish products; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; standards
Abstract: The Regulation deals with the Quality Standards of “Specialized Aquatic Goods” (SAG) either produced in the country or imported from abroad (art.3), the Dossiers related to all the activities concerning the aforesaid standards, the labeling of goods, and the classification of SAG Quality Standards “announcements” as per art. 9, the responsibilities of the manufacturers involved.

Title: Directive No. 07/2001/CT-BTS prohibiting the use of chloramphenicol and managing chemicals and veterinary drugs in the production of aquatic goods.
Keywords: aquaculture; fish products; drugs; fish diseases; animal health; crustaceans; food additives; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; Codex Alimentarius; toxicity/poisoning; residues
Abstract: The Directive bans the use of choramphenicol and regulates the use of other antibiotics in all the procedures related to the production, processing, preserving and handling of crustaceans and fish products. Moreover, it Decrees the necessity for inspections at production/processing sites, the reporting of results to the Ministry. This Directive is compliant with the Codex Alimentarius.

Title: Directive No. 07/2002/CT-TTg on enhancing the management over the use of antibiotics and chemicals in the production and trading of foodstuff with animal origin.
Keywords: animal feed/feedstuffs; drugs; animal health; fish products; cattle; residues; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; toxicity/poisoning
Abstract: The Directive of the Prime Minister deals with past complaints about the addition of forbidden agents to aquatic and agricultural products destined mainly to external trade. In particular, it reinforces the ban of assorted chemicals and antibiotics (i.e. chloramphenicol) from feedstuff for cattle, livestock, aquaculture, veterinary drugs and agents used in plant production or protection. Moreover, the ban includes also the use of aforesaid assorted chemicals and drugs in the processing, conservation and storage of products whatsoever. The Directive applies also to food stuff in order to protect consumers’ health. The Ministries of Aquatic resources, of Agriculture and Rural Development will assume the prime responsibility and will cooperate with the Ministry of Science, Technology and Environment and the Ministry of Health.

Title: Regulation on the control of residues of toxic and hazardous substances in reared aquatic animals and products thereof.
Keywords: fish products; residues; hygiene/sanitary procedures; toxicity/poisoning; food safety and quality; packaging/labelling; processing/handling
Abstract: The Decision regulates all the activities related to the detection of hazardous substances and the assessment of their potential toxicity in products deriving from aquaculture; the environmental planning to prevent and control eventual toxicity, the legal bodies supervising it and the handling of complains and violations.

Title: Regulation on conditions for ensuring food hygiene and safety in the production of, trading in, and use of, food additives.
Keywords: treated food; food additives; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; fraud; processing/handling; packaging/labelling
Abstract: The Regulation states the requirements in terms of production, use and trade of food additives as per art. 3 in order to comply with hygiene and safety procedures for foodstuff. Article 7 deals with hygiene requirements for workers to oblige with, the materials used in food dishware and cutlery, the labelling and packaging of food treated with food additives. Article 8 indicates which additive is banned from use, dosing and labelling for the approved ones. The Regulation addresses also some minor environmental issues, like the location far from polluted areas and polluting sources.

Title: Regulation on food safety conditions at collective refectories and eat-in kitchens as well as ready–made food ration processing business establishments.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; food additives; processing/handling
Abstract: The Regulation provides the guidelines for food safety and the application of hygienic measures to comply with, in terms of food processing, in the refectories and kitchens while preparing meals. It also applies to processing and handling out of ready-made rations.

Title: Circular No. 02/2002/TT-BTS guiding the implementation of Government’s Decree No. 86/2001 on the conditions for conducting aquatic resources production and business lines.
Keywords: aquaculture; processing/handling; hygiene/sanitary procedures; drugs; equipment; transport/storage; animal feed/feedstuffs; fish diseases; animal health; food safety and quality; fish products; HACCP
Abstract: The Circular rules for the implementation of Decree No. 86/2001/ND-CP dealing with the exploitation of aquatic resources, their production (aquaculture), their processing resulting in fish products to be consumed as food or as fishfeed, the environmental, veterinary and sanitary standards for production, and the requirements for facilities where they are produced/processed. Appendix I lists out the standards actually in use for conducting production and business of aquatic products. Implementations deal mostly with the exemption from the previous provisions contained in Decree No. 86/2001/ND-CP if and when same conditions apply to both individuals and manufacturers of aquatic resources; permits for their exploitation; provisional exploitation areas; production of aquatic breeds; provisions on environmental protection.

Title: Decision No. 28/QD-TTg approving the National Scheme on control of micro-organism contamination and chemical residues in foodstuffs in the period from 2003 to 2010.
Keywords: food additives; residues; standards; toxicity/poisoning; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; processing/handling; slaughtering; residues
Abstract: The Decree provides for preventing and control on the potential contamination with micro-organisms and chemical residues in foodstuff and their effects, applying a series of procedures to guarantee the safety and hygiene of all edible products from their cultivation to their processing as food stuff. It involves testing the foodstuff quality, hygiene and safety standards (FQHS) on centralized and decentralized laboratories, the legal and scientific documentations related to the system, the evaluation of the presence and usage of fertilizers, food additives and other possibly contaminating substances; training and regular updating of personnel involved in the activities of farming, breeding, slaughtering, processing edible products (included those deriving from aquaculture) and , furthermore, the identification of the authorities and Agencies responsible for the actuation of the scheme.

Title: Order No. 20/2003/L-CTN promulgating the Ordinance on food hygiene and safety.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; food additives; genetically modified organism (GMO); packaging/labelling; processing/handling; radiation; special dietary uses; transport/storage; treated food; fraud; residues; standards; toxicity/poisoning; residues; nutrition
Abstract: The Order includes the following: Chapter I, General Provisions; Chapter II, Food production and trading: Section 1- Fresh and raw food production and trading; Section 2- Food processing; Section 3- Food preservation and transportation; Section 4- Food import, export; Section 5- Food production and business conditions; Section 6- Announcement of food hygiene and safety standards; Section 7- Food advertisement and labelling. Chapter III, Prevention and overcoming of food poisoning and diseases transmitted via food; Chapter IV, State Management over food hygiene and safety. The Ordinance provides for the application of hygiene and safety measures in processing, packing, storing, transporting food either fresh or raw; it provides for the additives used in food processing, accidental poisoning, eventual food -transmitted diseases and the GMO technologies applied to food production.

Title: Circular No. 02/2004/TT-BTS guiding the implementation of Goverment's Decree No. 70/ND-CP stipulating the sanctioning of administrative violations in the aquatic resource domain.
Keywords: aquaculture; aquatic animals; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inland fisheries; mariculture; marine fisheries; offences/penalties; mariculture; drugs; fish diseases; animal health; food safety and quality; HACCP; residues
Abstract: The Circular provides for sanctioning of the following: violation of the protection of aquatic habitat and aquatic species; violation of the provisions on the exploitation of aquatic resources; violation of the provisions on fishing ship management; etc. Regulations regarding also environmental safety of fisheries, HACCP, residues contents, veterinary drugs, fishfeed, chemicals etc. and fishery lines. Sanctioning procedures are described also by providing sample forms and related documents.

Title: Circular No. 08/2004/TT-BYT guiding the management of functional foodstuff products.
Keywords: food additives; nutrition; packaging/labelling; special dietary uses; food safety and quality
Abstract: The Circular provides for foodstuff that has been added with special nutrients, vitamins and minerals and other assorted food additives meant to strengthen or support the body via nutritional implements. The RNI 2002 (Recommended Nutrient Intakes) should not be exceeded and in case of over dosage the labelling should clearly indicate the real dosages. In case of medically and biologically active ingredients, the supporting documentation proving their efficacy and intended use must be clearly indicated on the labels. The labels shall also indicate the instructions for use, together with effects, dosages, contraindications and any other important information related to the consumption of that particular foodstuff.

Title: Decree No.  163/2004/ND-CP detailing the implementation of a number of Articles of the Ordinance on Food Hygiene and Safety.
Keywords: food safety and quality; fraud; hygiene/sanitary procedures; internal trade; international trade; legal proceedings/administrative proceedings; packaging/labelling; processing/handling; toxicity/poisoning; treated food; HACCP; good practices
Abstract: The decree is divided in the following Chapters: I, General Provisions; II, Hygiene and safety in Food Production and Trading; III, State Management Responsibilities for Food Hygiene and Safety and responsibilities in the prevention and overcoming of food poisoning and food-borne diseases; IV, Examination and Inspection of Food Hygiene and Safety; V, Implementation Provisions. The provisions deal mostrly with the unalienating right of the customer to the choice, consumption and trading of healthy and safe food, whose hygiene and safety requirements are fullfilled and to be provided with information about food hygiene procedures in food processing, handling, packaging and selling. Poisoning and toxicity must be avoided at any cost as any contamination due to mis-handling or mis-processing by the personnel involved in food production.

Title: Decree No. 179/2004/ND-CP providing for State management over product and goods quality.
Keywords: food safety and quality; certification; institution; inspection; offences/penalties; governance
Abstract: This Decree provides for the implementation of the Ordinance on goods quality control (No. 18/1999/PL-UBTVQH10) and relevant law provisions on: State management over product and goods quality by State management agencies, organizations and individuals engaged; production and/or business organizations operating in the field of product and goods standard and quality control. The Decree consists of 39 articles divided into 9 Chapters: General provisions (I); Promulgation and application of product and goods quality standards (II); Quality certification and recognition of quality management system (III); Responsibilities of production business organizations and individuals for product and goods quality (IV); Assignment of responsibilities for State management over product and goods quality (V); Regulation on examination of product and goods quality (VI); Product and goods quality specialized inspectorate (VII); Sanctioning of law violations regarding product and goods quality (VIII); Implementation provisions (IX). The Decree applies to products and goods in the process of production and circulation on the domestic market as well as in import and export. The Ministry of Science and Technology shall assume the prime responsibility in formulating and promulgating the list of products and goods subject to the application of Vietnam standards related to food, hygiene, safety, human health, environment, etc.

Title: Decision No. 01/2005/QD-BYT promulgating the Regulation on food safety and hygiene conditions applicable to beverage establishments.
Keywords: beverages; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; packaging/labelling; transport/storage; inspection
Abstract: This Regulation prescribes conditions to ensure food safety and hygiene in the process of producing and processing beverage. The Regulation consists of 16 articles divided into 5 Chapters: General provisions (I); Provisions on beverage establishments (II); Responsibilities of establishment owners and provisions on people directly engaged in beverage production and processing (III); Provisions on packing, preservation, transportation and process of beverage production and processing (IV); Implementation provisions (V).

Title: Decision No. 02/2005/QD-BYT promulgating the Regulation on management of quality, hygiene and safety of bottled natural mineral water.
Keywords: mineral water; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; international trade; packaging/labelling; transport/storage; inspection
Abstract: This Regulation provides for the management of quality, safety and hygiene of bottled natural mineral water used for refreshment purposes. The Regulation consists of 22 articles divided into 7 Chapters: General provisions (I); Regulations on production and import of bottled natural mineral water (II); Regulations on hygienic conditions applicable to establishments producing bottled natural mineral water (III); Responsibilities of owners of establishments and regulations applicable to persons directly engaged in production (IV); Regulations on production process and technical regulations on bottled natural mineral water (V); Regulations on labelling, containing packages, preservation and transport (VI); Implementation provisions (VII). Article 3 defines bottled natural mineral water. Natural mineral water shall be processed by using one or more technical solutions as described in article 14. Attached to the Regulation are 2 Appendixes: Requirements regarding health safety related norms of bottled natural mineral water (1); Requirements regarding microorganisms for bottled natural mineral water (II).

Title: Decision No. 46/2005/QD-BNN promulgating a list of objects of veterinary hygiene inspection; a list of subjects liable to veterinary hygiene inspection; and a list of subjects liable to veterinary hygiene inspection and compulsory application of veterinary hygiene standards.
Keywords: animal feed/feedstuffs; animal health; drugs; hygiene/sanitary procedures; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; slaughtering
Abstract: This Decision promulgates a list of objects of veterinary hygiene inspection that include different kinds of bacteria, hormones, toxic gases, radioactive substances, antibiotics, etc. (sect. 1). The subjects liable to veterinary hygiene inspection shall include animals, animal products, animal feeds, etc. (sect. 2). Section 3 provides a list of subjects liable to veterinary hygiene inspection and to compulsory application of veterinary hygiene standards that include animals for slaughter, food, establishments which process and preserve animal products, means of transport for animal products, etc.

Title: Directive No. 37/2005CT-TTg on a number of measures to enhance the management of chemicals and antibiotics used in food production and trading.
Keywords: animal feed/feedstuffs; drugs; animal health; fish products; cattle; residues; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; toxicity/poisoning; processing/handling
Abstract: The aim of this Directive of the Prime Minister is to guarantee food hygiene and safety for consumers, and make Vietnamese products conformable to international standards. Furthermore, it prohibits the use of assorted chemicals and antibiotics in agricultural and aquatic products. Veterinary drugs and agents used for livestock shall not be used for aquatic animals and viceversa. The ban includes also the use of assorted chemicals and drugs in the processing, conservation and storage of products whatsoever. The Ministries of Trade will assume the prime responsibility and will cooperate with the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development, Fisheries, Health, Industry, Science and Technology and the Ministry of Natural Resources and Environment.

Title: Decision No. 42/2005/QD-BYT promulgating the Regulation on statement of food product specification.
Keywords: food safety and quality; food additives; hygiene/sanitary procedures; registration; inspection; internal trade; international trade; good practices
Abstract: This Regulation provides for procedures for the statement of food product specifications, testing and inspection, regime of fee collection, etc. The Regulation consists of 4 Chapters divided into 13 articles. Food products subject to specification statement are listed in article 1. Chapter II deals with procedures and dossiers' documentation necessary for the statement of product specifications which shall include: written statement of product specifications; table of standards set out by the trader; business registration certificates; slip of testing results on quality and hygienic criteria of the food subject to specification statement; labelled samples, etc. Responsibilities of traders are outlined in article 6. Traders shall apply for extension of valid duration of product specification statements as provided for in Chapter III. Chapter IV deals with testing, inspection and regime of fee collection and reporting.

Title: Decision No. 43/2006/QD-TTg Approving the National Action Plan for Food Safety and Hygiene until 2010.
Keywords: food safety and quality; HACCP; hygiene/sanitary procedures; policy/planning; institution; governance
Abstract: The purpose of this Decision is to approve the national action plan for food safety and hygiene, which shall have the following objectives: raise the awareness and responsibility of food producers, dealers and consumers towards food safety and hygiene; try to comply to world food standards; achieve the target that 100 percent of high-risk food production establishments apply the hazard analysis and critical control point (HACCP); prevent the spread through food of food poisoning and diseases; etc. Contents and implementation solutions of the national action plan, and implementation progress are described in article 1. The Inter-branch Steering Committee for Food Safety and Hygiene shall have the responsibility to direct, monitor, inspect and urge ministries, branches, etc., in effectively implementing this plan. Article 2 describes the responsibilities of each ministry in organizing the implementation of the plan.

Title: Decision No. 230/2006/QD-TTg approving the master plan on socio-economic development in Dien Bien province in the 2006-2020 period.
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Food & nutrition; Water
Keywords: agricultural development; farming; policy/planning; soil conservation/soil improvement; food safety and quality; food security; inland waters; irrigation; public water; sewerage; surface water; waste disposal; water supply
Abstract: This Decision approves the master plan on the socio-economic development of the Dien Bien province. The plan, among other things, shall: raise the forest coverage; solve the issues of environmental sanitation in urban centres, rural areas, etc.; assure food hygiene and safety; etc. Socio-economic development shall include: the development of agriculture, forestry and fishery; the stable development of food production; the use of water surface of lakes and ponds for aquaculture; the development of infrastructure systems including waterway traffic; the development of irrigation systems, and water supply and drainage systems.

Title: Decree No. 89/2006/ND-CP on labelling of goods.
Keywords: animal feed/feedstuffs; drugs; animal health; packaging/labelling; beverages; food additives; food safety and quality; international trade; fertilizers/nutrients; packaging/labelling; plant production
Abstract: This Decree provides for contents, ways of presentation and state management of labels of goods to be circulated in Vietnam, exported and imported. Goods shall include: food; drinks; food additives; feedstuff; veterinary drugs; plant protection drugs; plant seeds; fertilizers.

Title: Decision No. 161/QD-TTg approving the planning on salt production development up to 2010 and 2020.
Keywords: agricultural development; subsidy/incentive; condiments/herbs/spices
Abstract: This Decision approves the plan on salt production. The plan, in particular, shall: ensure efficient and sustainable salt production; meet people’s needs for consumer salt and industrial demands for material salt; to increase the export of salt and salt products, and reduce the import of industrial salt; and promote the development of the salt industry.

Title: Decision No. 04/2007/QD-BNN promulgating the Regulation on management of safe vegetables production and certification.
Keywords: ecological production/organic production; food safety and quality; vegetables/legumes; certification; MRL-maximum residue limit; pesticides; vegetables/legumes; ecological production/organic production; fertilizers/nutrients; integrated pest management-IPM/pest management strategies; pests/diseases; plant production; good practices; plant protection
Abstract: This Regulation lays down requirements for the production of safe vegetables, provides for the inspection and supervision of safe vegetable products, and for procedures for the certification of safe vegetables producing organizations. Safe vegetables are fresh vegetables which are produced, harvested, processed, packaged and preserved in accordance with the technical process to ensure that residues of toxic chemicals are below the permitted maximum levels. For the production of safe vegetables certain conditions shall be satisfied including: (a) cultivation land shall not be directly affected by wastes and shall have physical, chemical and biological characteristics that suit the growth of vegetables; (b) the use of organic fertilizers or fertilizers allowed for production and business in Viet Nam; (c) sources of water for irrigation must meet the standards for irrigating water and shall be inspected regularly; (d) the use of specific safe vegetables farming techniques; (e) the application of integrated pest management (IPM) techniques for disease prevention and elimination; etc.

Title: Decision No. 14/2007/QD-TTg approving the scheme on biotechnology development and application on the processing industry up to 2020.
Keywords: biotechnology; processing/handling; agricultural commodities
Abstract: This Decree approves the scheme on biotechnology development and application on the processing industry. The purpose of the scheme is to research into and create advanced biotechnologies in Viet Nam, in combination with importing modern technologies, for effective application to food processing and consumer goods production in order to better meet the domestic consumption and export demands.

Title: Decision No. 23/2007/QD-BYT promulgating the Regulation on state inspection of quality, hygiene and safety of imported foods.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection; certification; international trade; standards
Abstract: This Regulation lays down methods, contents and procedures for the inspection by state agencies of the quality and safety of food imported into Viet Nam. Imported foods may be sold in the Vietnamese market only after being granted a certificate of compliance with import requirements or a notice on inspection of dossier of an import shipment consisting of one or several product lots of the same goods or different types of product. The Regulation further provides for responsibilities and powers of inspection agencies, of imported goods consignees and of the Department for Food Safety and Hygiene.

Title: Decision No. 06/2007/QD-BTS adopting urgent measures to control residues of banned antibiotics and chemicals in Japan-bound seafood exports.
Keywords: drugs; fish diseases; animal health; food safety and quality; international trade; molluscs; crustaceans; food additives; residues
Abstract: This Decision provides for the adoption of urgent measures to control antibiotic and chemical residues in seafood exports to Japan. Only enterprises which meet food safety requirements of the Ministry of Fisheries shall be allowed to export seafood to Japan. The Decision further specifies the cases in which enterprises may not export crustacean and mollusc shipments to Japan. The authority in charge of implementing this Decision shall be the National Fisheries Quality Assurance and Veterinary Directorate.

Title: Decree No. 01/2008/ND-CP defining the functions, tasks, powers and organizational structure of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development.
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Livestock; Food & nutrition; Fisheries; Forestry; Cultivated plants
Keywords: institution; agricultural development; farming; financial agricultural measures; irrigation; land consolidation; less favoured areas; soil conservation/soil improvement; rural development; governance; animal health; drugs; institution; fishing authorization; fishery management and conservation; good practices; authorization/permit; governance; food safety and quality; forest management/forest conservation; seeds/planting material; plant production; plant protection
Abstract: This Decree provides for the functions, tasks, powers and organizational structure of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development. The Ministry of Agricultural and Rural Development is a governmental agency which shall perform function of state management in the domains of agriculture, plant protection, animal health, food quality, forestry, salt-making, fisheries, irrigation, rural development, etc. Tasks and powers of the Ministry are specified in the text.

Title: Order No. 14/2007/L-CTN on the promulgation of the Law on Product and Goods Quality.
Keywords: food safety and quality; inspection; certification; classification/declassification; dispute settlement; standards; fraud; packaging/labelling; liability/compensation; resource/damage valuation
Abstract: This Law provides for rights and obligations of organizations and individuals producing or trading in products and goods, and for the management of products and goods quality. Product and goods quality shall be controlled on the basis of applicable standards and relevant technical regulations in order to ensure safety to humans, animals, plants and the environment and to raise the productive quality and competitiveness of Viet Nam’s products and goods. The Law classifies products and goods according to their safety degree, and prescribes management methods for products and goods of any kind at different stages of production, importation and circulation. The Law further provides for the following: examination, inspection of product and goods quality; settlement of disputes, compensation for damage and handling of violations; responsibilities for state control of product and goods quality.

Title: Decision No. 10/2008/QD-TTg approving the strategy on animal breeding development up to 2020.
Keywords: animal feed/feedstuffs; animal health; animal production; drugs; good practices; food safety and quality; apiculture/sericulture; cattle; hygiene/sanitary procedures; international trade; poultry products; processing/handling; slaughtering; subsidy/incentive; swine
Abstract: This Decision aims at approving the strategy on animal breeding development. The purpose of the strategy is to: develop animal breeding into a commodity production industry by meeting domestic and export demands for food; ensure epidemic security and veterinary hygiene, and protect the environment; develop competitive animal breeding products such as pigs, poultry and cows; develop bee keeping and silkworm raising; develop the animal feed industry; and build suitable slaughter houses. Owners of farm- or industrial animal breeding establishments and industrial slaughtering, preserving and processing establishments shall be entitled to favourable conditions in land allocation and lease, and to favourable tax incentives. The Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development shall assume the prime responsibility for implementing the strategy.

Title: Decision No. 01/2008/QD-BNN providing for the labelling, ice-glazing and food additive use in frozen fish processing.
Keywords: fish products; food additives; food safety and quality; certification; international trade; packaging/labelling
Abstract: This Decision provides for the labelling and use of chemicals and food additives in the processing of frozen fish products. The labelling of frozen fish products shall comply with Viet Nam’s laws and requirements of importing countries, and shall fully and precisely specify the contents of the products. Food additives and ice-glazing shall also comply with requirements set by competent agencies of Viet Nam or importing countries. Goods that do not meet the requirements prescribed shall not be granted a quality certificate.

Title: Decision No. 53/2008/QD-BNN prohibiting acts of adding water or water-retaining additives to frozen fresh fish products in order to commit trade fraud.
Keywords: fish products; food additives; food safety and quality; inspection; treated food; fraud
Abstract: This Decision prohibits the addition of water or water-retaining additives to frozen fresh fish products. All enterprises which use equipment for spraying liquids or water-retaining additives shall report on the reasons for and purposes of the use of such equipment to the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development. The Decision assigns to the National Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries Quality Administrative Directorate of Viet Nam the prime responsibility for carrying out the provisions of this Act. In particular the Directorate shall conduct inspection to detect whether enterprises use water-retaining additives, check the content of water-retaining additives in frozen fresh fish products, and publicise the names of the enterprises that violate the provisions of this Decision.

Title: Decree No. 79/2008/ND-CP on the organizational system of management, inspection and assessment of food safety and hygiene.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection; internal trade; international trade
Abstract: This Decree provides for food safety management, inspection and testing systems from local to central levels. The Ministry of Health is the agency that performs the state management role in managing food safety and hygiene of domestic and imported food products. The Decree also specifies responsibilities of other ministries and of People’s Committees of provinces and centrally run cities regarding the food safety and hygiene, and provides for the organizational system of food safety and hygiene management. In addition, the Decree provides for the appointment of a food safety and hygiene inspectorate, defines its internal organization and its tasks and powers. The Decree further approves the establishment of the National Institute on Food Safety and Hygiene Assessment and some regional centres for food safety testing.

Title: Decision No. 41/2005/QD-BYT promulgating the Regulation on food safety and hygiene conditions for food trading and eating service establishments.
Keywords: food safety and quality; HACCP; hygiene/sanitary procedures; processing/handling; transport/storage; vending; internal trade
Abstract: This Regulation prescribes food safety and hygienic conditions for fixed and mobile food trading and eating service establishments. Facilities, equipment and tools for the processing, preservation and storage of food must meet these food safety and hygienic conditions.

Title: Decision No. 6289/2003 promulgating the Regulation on addition of micronutrients to food.
Keywords: food safety and quality; nutrition; packaging/labelling; transport/storage
Abstract: This Regulation sets out requirements for the addition of micronutrients to food. The Regulation provides for conditions that micronutrients must satisfy and set out requirements for producers, processors and traders of food added with micronutrients, and for the labelling, packing, preservation and transportation of this food. The Regulation further contains Appendixes specifying the micronutrients that may be added to different kinds of food.

Title: Decision No. 99/2008/QD-BNN promulgating the Regulation on management of safe vegetable, fruit and tea production and trading.
Keywords: coffee/cocoa/tea; consumer protection; fruits/edible nuts; hygiene/sanitary procedures; processing/handling; vegetables/legumes; certification; food safety and quality; beverages; coffee/cocoa/tea; hygiene/sanitary procedures; plant production; soil pollution/quality; vegetables/legumes; good practices
Abstract: This Regulation prescribes conditions and certification of eligibility for safe vegetable, fruit and tea production and preliminary processing. The Regulation sets out requirements and procedures for filing registration applications for certificates of eligibility for safe vegetable, fruit and tea production and preliminary processing. The Regulation further provides for the following: conditions for trading in safe vegetables, fruit and tea; inspection measures; handling of violations committed by producers or by certified organizations; sampling and sample testing of soil and water, and of vegetable, fruit and tea products; etc. Furthermore, the Regulation contains three Appendices specifying the maximum allowable limits of heavy metals in soil and water and the maximum allowable limits of microorganisms and harmful chemicals in vegetable, fruit and tea products.

Title: Decision No. 38/2008/QD-BYT promulgating the Regulation on limits of cross-contaminated melamine in food.
Keywords: food safety and quality; fraud
Abstract: This Regulation sets limits of unintentional direct contamination with melamine of food products, prohibits intentional adulteration of food with melamine and sets out methods to determine melamine in food.

Title: Decision No. 118/2008/QD-BNN promulgating the Regulation on inspection and certification of food quality, hygiene and safety of fisheries goods.
Keywords: food safety and quality; inspection; certification; certification; residues
Abstract: This Regulation provides for the order and procedures of inspection and certification of food quality, hygiene and safety of fisheries goods. The Regulation applies to fisheries goods for domestic consumption or export, and imported fisheries goods. The Regulation entrusts the Agriculture and Rural Development Ministry to appoint inspection agencies. Inspections may be mandatory when inspection activities are carried out according to state management requirements or they may be requested by product owners. Products originating from specified establishments as provided for in the text shall be exempt from inspection. Procedures and requirements to obtain a safety control certificate are set out in the text. The Regulation further provides for: requirements for the labelling and packaging of products; duties and powers of owners of products and inspecting agencies; notification of inspection results; inspection and certification charges and fees; settlement of complaints and handling of violations; etc.

Title: Decree No. 132/2008/ND-CP detailing the implementation of a number of articles of the Law on Product and Goods Quality.
Keywords: food safety and quality; inspection; international trade; internal trade; processing/handling; subsidy/incentive
Abstract: This Decree implements a number of articles of the Law on Product and Good's Quality in relation to: the control of the quality of imported, exported or domestic products and goods; control of the quality of products during the production process; examination of products' and goods' quality and quality controllers; national quality awards; and responsibilities for state control of product and good's quality.

Title: Circular No. 56/2009/TT-BNNPTNT on examination and inspection of aquatic food hygiene and safety before market circulation.
Keywords: aquatic animals; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection; internal trade; transport/storage; vending
Abstract: This Circular provides for the examination and inspection of aquatic food hygiene and safety before its circulation on the market. The Circular prescribes powers and responsibilities of examination and inspection agencies and sets out conditions for aquatic products in order to be sold on the market. Examination and inspection shall be done during the entire process from fishing, preservation and transportation to landfall of fishing vessels, at aquaculture or harvesting establishments, at fishing ports, at aquatic material purchasing and preserving establishments, at fish markets, etc. The Circular further provides for the elaboration of a national program on post-harvest inspection of aquatic food hygiene and safety.

Title: Circular No. 30/2009/TT-BNNPTNN prescribing veterinary hygiene examination and control of animal product production and trading and slaughter of animals for food.
Keywords: animal feed/feedstuffs; animal health; animal production; drugs; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; international trade; slaughtering
Abstract: This Circular provides for the examination and control of animal products for trade, the slaughter of animals and for the examination, control and use of veterinary drugs and feed in the raising of animals. The Animal Health Department is responsible for elaborating annual plans on veterinary hygiene examination and control of animal products for export, import and domestic consumption. The plans shall be submitted to the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development for approval and allocation of funds for their implementation. The Circular provides for restrictions on the use of veterinary drugs and feed in the raising of animals. The Circular further provides for the handling of violations and for implementation responsibilities of agencies.

Title: Resolution No. 34/2009/NQ-QH12 on stepping up the implementation of policies and laws on food quality, hygiene and safety administration.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection; international trade; standards; investment
Abstract: This Resolution provides for the implementation of policies and laws on food quality, hygiene and safety administration. The Government, in order to implement such policies and laws, must perform the following tasks: promptly submit to the National Assembly for consideration the Law on Food Safety; formulate a national strategy for food quality, hygiene and safety assurance; strengthen the system of state management agencies in charge of food quality, hygiene and safety assurance; intensify direction and administration work of state management responsibilities for food quality, hygiene and safety at the stages of production of raw materials, preliminary working, processing, import, export and supply of food; strengthen the inspection system; allocate sufficient funds for food quality, hygiene and safety administration; increase investment in the development of national standards and national and local technical regulations; encourage disease-free and environmentally friendly farm models to produce safe food in association with developing safe agricultural commodity markets; etc.

Title: Circular No. 05/2010/TT-BNNPTNT guiding the hygiene and safety inspection and control of agricultural food before market circulation.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection
Abstract: This Circular provides for the hygiene and safety inspection and control of all vegetable- and animal-based products or processed foodstuffs used as food intended for humans before market sale. The Circular provides for: hygiene and safety inspection grounds and principles, and for agencies responsible for guiding and supervising the inspection; implementation of agricultural food hygiene and safety control programs at stages of plantation, breeding, slaughtering, processing, packaging and sale; remedies when detecting the contamination of microorganisms or chemicals in excess of prescribed limits; agencies and institutions responsible for the implementation of this Circular.

Title: Circular No. 25/2010/TT-BNNPTNT guiding the food hygiene and safety inspection of imports of animal origin.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection; international trade; fees/royalties
Abstract: This Circular guides the food hygiene and safety inspection of imports of animal origin under the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development’s management, which include food products of terrestrial and aquatic animal origin. The Circular specifies food hygiene and safety inspection procedures in exporting countries and of import lots in Viet Nam. The Circular further provides for: measures to handle violations of food hygiene and safety regulations; charges, fees and funds for inspection and control of products; responsibilities of the National Agro-Forestry-Fisheries Quality Assurance Department, the Animal Health Department, etc.

Title: Circular No. 13/2010/TT-BYT guiding the management of quick food test kits.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; packaging/labelling; processing/handling; registration
Abstract: This Circular provides for the experimentation, registration for the circulation, the advertisement, labelling and use of quick food test kits in order to control hygiene and safety in the process of food production, processing and trading. All enterprises producing, trading in, importing or distributing testing chemicals and instruments shall request circulation registration certificates of quick food test kits.

Title: Decision No. 775/QD-TTg approving the detailed master plan on radiation development and application in agriculture through 2020.
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Livestock; Food & nutrition; Fisheries; Cultivated plants
Keywords: agricultural development; radiation; soil conservation/soil improvement; emissions; radiation; emissions; aquaculture; radiation; emissions; agricultural commodities; food security; plant variety; radiation; fertilizers/nutrients; plant protection; plant production; emissions
Abstract: This Decision approves the detailed master plan on radiation development and application in agriculture aiming at contributing to sustainable agricultural development along the line of producing commodities with high productivity, quality, efficiency and competitiveness and at assuring food security. Radiation development and application in agriculture covers: the selection and creation of plant varieties resistant to major harmful insects and severe conditions and suitable to different eco-zones; the selection and creation of new varieties of microorganisms for producing fertilizers and environmental treatment preparations and protecting plants, livestock and fisheries; the use of radioactive isotopes for studying and managing soil nutrients for a number of plants, studying soil erosion and producing soil reconditioning bio-materials and fertilizers; the use of radioactive isotopes for assessing residues of heavy metals in aquaculture and treating environmental pollution in consolidated aquaculture zones; etc. The Decision further specifies principal solutions for reaching the objectives of the plan and the ministries and state agencies responsible for implementing this plan.

Title: Circular No. 24/2010/TT-BCT on the application of regulations on automatic import licensing to a number of good items.
Keywords: authorization/permit; international trade; meat; sugar; vegetables/legumes; beverages; cereals/grains/rice; coffee/cocoa/tea; fish products; agricultural commodities
Abstract: This Circular provides for the grant of automatic import licences for goods including food items. The Ministry of Industry and Trade shall grant automatic import licences to traders by certifying the import registration application for each lot. The licence shall be valid for 30 days from the date of its certification. The Circular further provides for: application for automatic import licences; time limits for granting automatic import licences; regulations on automatic import licences upon carrying out custom procedures; etc.

Title: Circular No. 45/2010/TT-BNNPTNT providing for conditions on food safety and hygiene-guaranteed intensive tiger shrimp- and white-leg shrimp-rearing establishments and zones.
Keywords: crustaceans; hygiene/sanitary procedures; processing/handling; aquaculture; fish diseases; drugs; animal feed/feedstuffs; animal health; aquatic animals; crustaceans; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; waste management
Abstract: This Circular provides for the conditions on food safety and hygiene-guaranteed intensive tiger shrimp- and white-leg shrimp-rearing establishments and zones. Shrimp rearing-establishments and zones must lie within planned regions, comply with local regulations on shrimp farming, be numbered and registered under regulations of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development and satisfy water resource quality requirements set out in Appendix I. Furthermore, rearing establishments and zones must comply with: conditions on infrastructure; conditions on special-use equipment, machinery and instruments; conditions on shrimp rearing technological process which include selection of breeds, feeds and feed supplements, waste water and wastes, disease prevention for shrimps and requirements on product harvesting; and conditions on file management.

Title: Circular No. 44/2010/TT-BNNPTNT providing for conditions on food safety and hygiene-guaranteed intensive Asian catfish-rearing establishments and zones.
Keywords: inland fisheries; hygiene/sanitary procedures; processing/handling; aquaculture; fish diseases; drugs; animal feed/feedstuffs; animal health; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; waste management
Abstract: This Circular provides for the management of Asian catfish-rearing establishments and zones. The Circular provides for conditions that must be met by Asian catfish rearing establishments and zones, including: they must lie within planned regions, comply with local regulations on shrimp farming and be numbered and registered under regulations of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development; satisfy water resource quality requirements set out in Appendix I; and comply with conditions on infrastructure, special-use equipment, machinery and instruments, and on Asian catfish rearing technological process which include selection of breeds, feeds and feed supplements, waste water and wastes, disease prevention for fish and requirements on product harvesting.

Title: Law No. 55/2010/QH12 on Food Safety.
Keywords: basic legislation; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; internal trade; international trade; packaging/labelling; processing/handling; transport/storage; genetically modified organism (GMO); residues; good practices; fraud; packaging/labelling
Abstract: This Law provides for rights and obligations of organizations and individuals in assuring food; conditions for assuring safety of foods and food production, trade, transport, import and export; food advertisement and labelling; food testing; food safety risk analysis; prevention of food safety incidents; food safety information, education and communication; and for responsibilities of state management of food safety. The Law provides for food safety assurance conditions for fresh and raw food, processed food, micronutrient-fortified food, functional food, genetically modified food, irradiated food, food additives, and for food-packaging tools and food packages and containers. The Law further sets out conditions, procedures and requirements for establishments engaged in food production and trade to obtain a certificate of food safety eligibility.

Title: Decree No. 109/2010/ND-CP on rice export business.
Keywords: cereals/grains/rice; international trade; certification; agricultural commodities
Abstract: This Decree provides for the commercial export of paddies and rice of all categories. Vietnamese traders of all economic sectors must satisfy the conditions specified in this Decree and obtain a certificate to conduct rice export business. Procedures and requirements for obtaining a certificate by the Ministry of Industry and Trade are set out in the text. The Decree provides for objectives and principles that the administration of rice export must adhere to, including: the increase of commodity rice sale and assurance of interests of rice growers under current policy; balance of export and domestic consumption; valorization of domestic rice prices; and fulfilment of international commitments. The Decree further provides for responsibilities of state agencies and concerned organizations and individuals, and for violations and their handling.

Title: Circular No. 44/2010/TT-BCT detailing a number of articles of Government’s Decree No. 109/2010/ND-CP of 4 November 2010 on rice export business.
Keywords: cereals/grains/rice; international trade; registration; agricultural commodities
Abstract: This Circular implements various articles of Government’s Decree No. 109 of 2010 on rice export business. The Circular sets out procedures for the registration of rice export contracts. It provides for the application of floor export prices of rice and for information contained in rice export contracts. The Circular further provides for: designation of key traders; responsibilities of the Vietnam Food Association; criteria for the allocation of entrusted export quotas; hiring of rice warehouses and mills to meet rice export requirements; etc.

Title: Circular No. 03/2011/TT-BNNPTNT on tracing and recall of fishery products failing to meet food quality and safety requirements.
Keywords: fish products; food safety and quality; traceability
Abstract: This Circular provides principles and procedures for the tracing and recall of fishery products which do not meet food quality and safety requirements. All the fishery production and business establishments specified in the Circular shall establish a tracing system meeting the requirements listed in the Appendix and shall adopt a policy to recall lots of delivery failing to meet food quality and safety requirements in the cases set out in article 6. The Circular further provides for responsibilities of organizations and individuals engaged in fishery production and business and for agencies involved in the implementation of the Circular.

Title: Decision No. 809/QD-TTg approving the Scheme on improvement of the capacity for quality management of agricultural, forestry and fishery products and salt for the 2011-2015 period.
Keywords: food safety and quality; condiments/herbs/spices; fish products; policy/planning; standards
Abstract: This Decision approves the Scheme on improvement of the capacity for quality management of agricultural, forestry and fishery products and salt. The objectives of the Scheme are to: consolidate and improve the capacity for management and control of the food quality and safety of agricultural, forestry and fishery products and salt; perfect the system of legal documents on management of food quality and safety of agricultural, forestry and fishery products and salt, ensuring their conformity with domestic and international standards and commitments; and to build advanced and modern infrastructure to meet management and integration requirements in a new period.

Title: Circular No. 32/2011/TT-BCT amending and supplementing Circular No. 24/2010/TT-BCT of 28 May 2010 on the application of regulations on automatic import licensing to a number of goods items.
Keywords: authorization/permit; international trade
Abstract: This Circular amends Circular No. 24/2010/TT-BCT on the application of regulations on automatic import licensing to a number of goods items by inserting in article 1 imported goods which shall not be regulated by provisions of the Circular.

Title: Circular No. 28/2011/TT-BYT amending and supplementing a number of articles of Circular No. 13/2010/TT-BYT of 12 May 2010 guiding the management of quick food test kits.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; packaging/labelling; processing/handling; registration
Abstract: This Circular amends and supplements articles 9, 10 and 11 of  Circular No. 13/2010/TT-BYT guiding the management of quick food test kits, which provide for the registration for the circulation of quick food test kits and for the issuance of circulation registration certificates.

Title: Decree No. 99/2011/ND-CP detailing and guiding a number of articles of the Law on Protection of Consumer Rights.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; consumer protection
Abstract: This Decree details and guides a number of articles of the Law on Protection of Consumer Rights. The Decree provides for: the protection of consumers in transactions with persons engaged in independent and regular commercial activities not subject to business registration; contracts with consumers and general transaction conditions; settlement of consumer right protection requests; social organizations engaged in consumer right protection; mediation of disputes between consumers and institutional or individual traders; and state management of consumer right protection.

Title: Circular No. 74/2011/TT-BNNPTNT on traceability, recall and handling of unsafe agricultural and forest food.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; processing/handling; traceability
Abstract: This Circular provides for the traceability, recall and handling of unsafe agricultural and forest food and for responsibilities of organization and persons engaged in the production and trade of agricultural and forest food.

Title: Circular No. 75/2011/TT-BNNPTNT providing the registration and certification of food advertisement contents under the management of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development.
Keywords: certification; food safety and quality; packaging/labelling
Abstract: This Circular provides for procedures, requirements and competences for obtaining food advertisement content certification. The Circular further provides for responsibilities and powers of establishment s registering certification of food advertisement contents, of standing agencies, related units under the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development, and of Provincial-level Agriculture and Rural Development Departments.

Title: Decision No. 20/QD-TTg approving the national food safety strategy for the 2011-2020 period, with a vision toward 2030.
Keywords: food safety and quality; consumer protection; fraud; processing/handling; good practices
Abstract: This Decision approves the national food safety strategy to control food safety from production to consumption in order to protect health and rights of consumers and to meet the requirements of national development and international integration of the country. The strategy shall aim at raising awareness and practicing food safety for target groups; building capacity for food safety management system; improving food safety assurance in manufacture, processing and selling facilities; actively preventing acute food poisoning. The strategy provides for major solutions to reach its aims including pointing out 3 core intervention groups which are: an intervention group on execution and direction; intervention group on technical specialization; and an intervention group on resources. Prime responsibility for the implementation and execution of this Decision shall be taken on by the Ministry of Health in cooperation with other ministries and sectors.

Title: Decision No. 226/QD-TTg approving the national nutrition strategy for the 2011-2020 period, with a vision toward 2030.
Keywords: nutrition; research; food safety and quality; education; policy/planning; food fortification
Abstract: This Decision approves the national nutrition strategy for the 2011-2020 period, with a  vision toward 2030 in order to increase in quantity people’s diet which shall be more balanced in quality and safe and hygienic; reduce dramatically child malnutrition; effectively control overweight and obesity to reduce nutrition-related chronic diseases. The Strategy shall implement key activities for nutrition improvement, paying attention to education, training and human resources development and supplementation of policies to support nutrition. The Ministry of Health shall assume the prime responsibility for planning and organizing the strategy implementation nationwide. Other ministries responsible for organizing the implementation of this strategy are listed in the text.

Title: Decree No. 38/2012/ND-CP detailing the implemenation of a number of articles of the Law on Food Safety.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; international trade; packaging/labelling; genetically modified organism (GMO); certification; processing/handling; inspection
Abstract: This Decree implements provisions of the Law on Food Safety. The Decree makes provision for the: announcement of regulation conformity or announcement of conformity with regulations on food safety; safety assurance of food with ingredients from genetically modified organisms; grant and revocation of certificates of food safety eligibility for establishments; state inspection of food safety for imported or exported food; food labelling; assignment of responsibilities for state management of food safety; and specialized inspection of food safety.

Title: Decree No. 63/2012/ND-CP defining the functions, tasks, powers and organizational structure of the Ministry of Health.
Keywords: institution; pollution control; governance; institution; food safety and quality; governance
Abstract: This Decree provides for the functions, tasks, powers and organizational structure of the Ministry of Health. The Ministry of Health is a governmental agency performing the state management of health, covering preventive health, and among other things also food safety and environmental protection. Powers and task of the Ministry regarding food safety and environmental protection are outlined in detail in the Decree.

Title: Circular No. 16/2012/TT-BYT providing food safety conditions for producers and traders of food products, food-packaging tools and food packages and containers under the management of the Ministry of Health.
Keywords: food safety and quality; certification; food additives; mineral water; packaging/labelling; transport/storage; inspection
Abstract: This Circular provides for food safety conditions for producers and traders of functional food, micronutrient-fortified food, food additives, food processing aids, natural mineral water, bottled drinking water, food packaging tools containers; etc. The Circular provides food safety conditions in food preservation and food transportation, and it regulates the inspection of food producers and traders.

Title: Circular No. 29/2012/TT-BCT providing the grant and revocation of food safety certificates for establishments under the management of the Ministry of Industry and Trade.
Keywords: food safety and quality; certification
Abstract: This Circular provides application forms for food safety certificates for establishments engaged in food production and trade. The Circular sets out procedures and requirements for applying for food safety certificates and provides for the competence of the Ministry of Industry and Trade to grant the certificates.

Title: Circular No. 26/2012/TT-BYT on grant of certificates of establishments eligible for food safety for producers and tracers of functional food, micronutrient-fortified food, food additives or food processing aids; natural mineral water or bottled drinking water; and food packaging tools and materials and food containers under the Ministry of Health’s management.
Keywords: business/industry/corporations; food safety and quality; processing/handling; certification; food additives; mineral water; packaging/labelling
Abstract: This Circular provides for dossiers, procedures and competences to grant certificates of establishments eligible for food safety to producers and traders of functional food, micronutrient-fortified food, food additives or food processing aids, natural mineral water or bottled drinking water, packaging and tools in direct contact with food.

Title: Order No. 24/2012/L-CTN on the promulgation of the Law on the National Reserve No. 22/2012/QH13.
Keywords: hazards; institution; governance; hazards; food security
Abstract: This Law regulates the use of national reserves in order to cope with natural disasters and epidemics or national security. It provides regulations on the formation, management, steering and use of the national reserve, and the rights and duties of organizations, agencies and individuals associated with national reserve activities. The list of national reserve commodities covers food and plant seeds, among others. According to the law, the State sets up and uses the national reserve to meet the urgent demands for the prevention and control of natural calamities, fire, epidemics as well as recovery work and to serve national defence and security.

Title: Circular No. 59/2012/TT-BNNPTNT on management of safe vegetable, fruit and tea production.
