Keywords: coffee/cocoa/tea; consumer protection; fruits/edible nuts; hygiene/sanitary procedures; processing/handling; vegetables/legumes; certification; food safety and quality; beverages; coffee/cocoa/tea; hygiene/sanitary procedures; plant production; soil pollution/quality; vegetables/legumes
Abstract: This Regulation prescribes conditions and certification of food safety eligibility for vegetable, fruit and tea production, pre-processing and processing establishments. The Regulation sets out requirements and procedures for filing registration applications for certificates of eligibility for safe vegetable, fruit and tea production pre-processing and processing establishments. The Regulation further provides for the following: conditions for trading in safe vegetables, fruit and tea; inspection measures; handling of violations; etc.

Title: Circular No. 19/2012/TT-BYT guiding the regulation conformity announcement and food safety regulation conformity announcement.
Keywords: food safety and quality; food additives; hygiene/sanitary procedures; registration; inspection; internal trade; international trade
Abstract: This Circular stipulates procedures and dossiers for regulation conformity announcement and food safety regulation conformity announcement applicable to packed processed food, food additives, food-processing supporting substances, packaging materials and instruments in direct contact with food. Organizations and individuals are required to make self-announcement that their products are in conformity with regulations on food safety if technical regulations applicable to announced products are not available. The Circular also requires individuals/organizations who would like to do their product announcement, to implement product testing at the designated laboratory or independent laboratory prior to application for announcement.

Title: Decision No. 950/QD-TTg promulgating the action programme for implementation of the goods import and export strategy for 2011-2020 (toward 2030).
Keywords: agricultural development; international trade; vegetables/legumes; cereals/grains/rice; coffee/cocoa/tea; fruits/edible nuts; policy/planning; aquatic animals; food safety and quality; beverages; agricultural commodities
Abstract: This Decision, consisting of 5 Chapters, promulgates the action programme for implementation of the agricultural goods import and export strategy for 2011-2020 (toward 2030). This Decision applies for economic restructuring, identification of potential export items, formulating mechanisms, implementing policies and finalizing strategies to develop agricultural production, in particular: rice, coffee, tea, pepper, cashew nut, rubber and aquatic products.

Title: National Nutrition Strategy for 2011 – 2020, with a Vision toward 2030.
Keywords: nutrition; food security; education; capacity building; policy/planning; research; institution; early warning system; food safety and quality; public health; breastfeeding/infant food; school feeding; governance; food fortification
Abstract: This National Nutrition Strategy for 2011 – 2020, is a multi-sectoral policy document, aiming to improve the diet of Vietnamese people in terms of quantity and quality, reduce malnutrition of children, and manage obesity/overweight. The vision to 2030 is to reduce child malnutrition below the level of public health significance and to remarkably increase the mean height in adults. In addition, increased awareness about proper nutrition and behavior change should be improved in the general population for the prevention of nutrition related chronic diseases and adequate food safety controls should be ensured. Concerning helping eliminate hunger, food insecurity and malnutrition, the National Nutrition Strategy (NNS) seeks to provide focus and directions to the National Nutrition Programs and development partners. The main objectives are: to improve the nutrition status of mothers and children; to continue to improve the diet of Vietnamese people, in terms of quantity and quality; to improve micro-nutrient status; to reinforce capacity and effectiveness of the network of nutrition services in both community and health care facilities; to improve knowledge and practices regarding proper nutrition in the general population. Main projects /programs include: national program for food and nutrition security and nutrition in emergencies, with priority given to disadvantaged areas, ethnic minorities, and areas with high prevalence of undernutrition; project for nutrition education, communication and capacity building; project for maternal and child malnutrition control, focused on reduction of stunting, improvement of height, and proper health and nutrition for pregnant women; project for micronutrient deficiency control; program for school nutrition. Nutrition activities should involve multiple sectors, under the guidance and leadership of the Government at all levels, with social mobilisation of mass organisations and the general population. Priority should be given to poor, disadvantaged areas and ethnic minority groups, and for mothers and small children. A legislative framework should be developed and finalised to deal with food and nutrition issues. Concerning enabling more inclusive and efficient agricultural and food systems, the key elements addressed by the NSS include: Local food production, processing and utilization should be promoted and diversified; the Vegetation - Aquaculture - Cage for Animal husbandry (VAC) ecosystem should be further developed, ensuring the production, circulation and distribution of safe foods; research and disseminate information regarding post-harvest technology, with special regards to household scale, should be promoted; research, development and technology applications should be promoted to develop creation and selection of new breeds of livestock, production and processing of nutritionally fortified foods and specialized products. The NNS recognizes climate change and natural disasters are challenges affecting the nutrition situation. Vietnam has been considered as one among 5 countries badly affected by global climate change. Each year, Vietnam faces many natural disasters including floods, typhoons, and drought, leading to increased risk of serious epidemics and food insecurity. The NNS addresses this issue through the national program for food and nutrition security and nutrition in emergencies. Main activities include: develop an early warning surveillance system to identify risks of food and nutrition insecurity, as well as a food price variability surveillance system; and develop a plan of action for timely response in emergency situations. The Ministry of Health shall be the focal point, assisting the government to manage the NNS through coordination, direction, supervision, monitoring, and evaluation, periodically reporting on progress and operational results within the government. Other relevant ministries, sectors, localities, political and social organisations are responsible for submitting an annual report to the MOH, who will review on their progress and operational results in achieving the NNS objectives.

Title: Comprehensive Poverty Reduction and Growth Strategy (CPRGS).
Keywords: poverty; public health; social protection; food security; business/industry/corporations; public participation; governance; education; public health; financial agricultural measures; equity; gender; hazards; cultural heritage; poverty; public health; nutrition; social protection; food security; hygiene/sanitary procedures; food safety and quality; business/industry/corporations; public participation; governance; education; public health; financing; equity; gender
Abstract: The present Comprehensive Poverty Reduction and Growth Strategy considers poverty reduction to be the basic element to ensure social justice and sustainable growth; and in-return, high and sustainable growth could bring about physical energy to support and provide opportunities for the poor to get out of poverty holes. In Part II it is repeated that it is essential to bring Vietnam out of the state of underdevelopment, to significantly improve the people’s material, cultural and spiritual life; and to lay the foundations for transforming the country into a modern industrialized country by 2020. In particular, to enhance the human resources, scientific and technological capacity, infrastructure, and economic, defense, and security potential; to establish the institutions of a socialist-oriented market economy; to advance Vietnam’s position in the international arena. In order to achieve these objectives, in Part III it is suggested to: 1) Create a legal environment that support fair and competitive businesses; 2) Reform continously State-Owned Enterprises (SOEs); 3) Create conditions for land use, support business production and develop small and medium-size enterprises, farms and various types of enterprises in the private sector; 4) Encourage foreign investment and consider it a long-term component of the national economy; Implement the Public Administration Reform (PAR), Legal Reform and pro-poor Good Governance. Furthermore, the macroeconomic stability shall be maintained and a social environment shall be created to bring about social equality, enhance grassroots democracy and provide legal support to the poor.Part IV considers the large scale infrastructure development for growth and poverty reduction and establishes that large-scale infrastructure plays an important role in the process of growth and poverty reduction, which depends on the natural, social and economic conditions of each locality. Part V lays down provisions relating to major policies and measures to develop sectors and industries to promote sustainable growth and poverty reduction. It is envisaged to: 1) Develop agriculture and rural economy to achieve rapid poverty reduction; 2) develop industry and urban areas in order to create more jobs and improve the living standards of the poor; 3) Develop infrastructure to create opportunities for poor areas, poor communes and poor people to access public services; 4) Establish an education system for the people that is more egalitarian and of higher quality; 5) Improve the quality of health services, family planning programmes, improve access and reduce health care costs for the poor; 6) Develop an advanced culture, strong national identity; improve the dissemination of information to help the people expand their options; 7) Protect the environment and maintain a healthy life for the poor. In particular, the Strategy is formulated in 6 Parts as follows: Part I provides for: I) Socio-Economic Setting; II) Current Poverty Situation and Causes of Poverty in Vietnam; III) Achievement and Challenges. Part II concerns: I) Overall Objectives and Tasks of Socio-EconomicDevelopment and Poverty Reduction; II) Major Socio-Economic Development and Poverty Reduction Targets for the Period up to 2005 and 2010; III) The Relationship between Growth and Poverty Reduction. Part III takes into consideration: I) Creating a Legal Environment that Supports Fair and Competitive Business; II) Maintain the Macro-Economic Stability; III) Create a Social Environment to bring about Social Equality, Enhance Grassroots Democracy and Provide Legal Support to the Poor. Part IV deals with: I) the Role of Large-Scale Infrastructre for Growth and Poverty Reduction; II) Evaluation of current State of Large-Scale Infrastructure in Vietnam; III) Objectives and Directions of Large-Scale Infrastructure aiming at Growth and Poverty Reduction; IV) Policies and Solutions for Large-Scale Infrastructure Development. Part V lays down provisions relating to: I) Policies and Measures to Develop Major Sectors and Industries to Create Income Generation Opportunities for the Poor; II) Narrow the Gap between Different Regions, Realize Gender Equality and the Advancement of Women; III) Development of Social Safety Nets for the Poor and for Victims of Natural Disasters. Part VI specifies: I) Investment for Development; II) State Budget Expenditure (Public Expenditure); III) Poverty Reduction. Part VII sets out: I) Implementation; II) Monitoring and Evaluation of Implementation.

Title: Circular No. 40/2012/TT-BCT on grant of food advertisement content certificates under the management of the Ministry of Industry and Trade.
Keywords: food safety and quality; processing/handling; certification; food additives; packaging/labelling; beverages; milk/dairy products; internal trade; international trade
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 14 articles divided into three Chapters, provides for the competence, order and procedures for certification of advertisement contents being names, ingredients, uses, use instructions, preservation and effect warnings (if any) of one or a group of the following kinds of food: liquor, beer, soft drink, processed milk, vegetable oil, processed flour and starch products, confectionery and jam. This Circular applies to: 1. Organizations and individuals producing, processing, packaging and trading in liquor, beer, soft drink, processed milk, vegetable oil, processed flour and starchy products, confectionery and jam which are advertised in the Vietnamese territory.  2. Other related agencies, organizations and individuals. Article 3 establishes the conditions for obtaining advertisement content certificates: 1. The establishment has a complete and valid registration dossier as prescribed in article 8. 2. The food products are outside the list of products and goods banned from advertising under current law. 3. The establishment's food products meet food safety requirements or are of establishments/countries on the list of those recognized by competent Vietnamese agencies to fully meet food safety requirements under current law. 4. Advertisement contents are accurate and true to the quality and food safety assurance as announced and registered.

Title: Circular No. 45/2012/TT-BCT providing in ­process inspection of food quality and safety under the state management of the Ministry of Industry and Trade.
Keywords: food safety and quality; inspection; hygiene/sanitary procedures; packaging/labelling; food additives; milk/dairy products; beverages
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 17 articles divided into four Chapters, provides the principles, order and procedures for in-process inspection of food quality and safety under the state management of the Ministry of Industry and Trade, including: 1. Beer-liquor-soft drink; 2. Processed milk; 3. Vegetable oil; 4. Processed flour and starch products; 5. Confectionery and jam; 6. Packages and containers of the products specified at Points 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5 of this article. This Circular applies to: 1. Organizations and individuals producing food products above specified  and having registered relevant production and business lines in accordance with Vietnam's law. 2. Other related agencies, organizations and individuals. This Circular is divided as follows: General Provisions (Chap. I); Order and Procedures for In­-Process Inspection of Food Quality and Safety (Chap. II); Responsibilities and Powers of Inspection Agencies, Inspection Teams and Related Organizations and Individuals (Chap. III); Implementation Provisions (Chap. IV). The Appendix VII lists Production Establishments Subject to Food Quality and Safety Inspection by the Ministry of Industry and Trade.

Title: Circular No. 39/2012/TT-BCT detailing a number of articles of the Government's Decree No. 94/2012/ND-CP on liquor production and trading.
Keywords: food safety and quality; inspection; hygiene/sanitary procedures; packaging/labelling; authorization/permit; certification; viticulture/oenological practices
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 18 articles divided into six Chapters, provides dossiers and procedures for granting, re-granting, modifying, supplementing and revoking licenses for industrial production of liquor, licenses for small-scale production of liquor for sale, certificates of registration of small-scale production of liquor for sale to enterprises licensed to produce liquor for further processing, licenses for liquor distribution, licenses for liquor wholesale, licenses for liquor retail as prescribed in the Government's Decree No. 94/2012/ND-CP of 12 November 2012, on liquor production and trading (below referred to as Decree No. 94/2012/ND-CP). This Circular applies to domestic and foreign organizations and individuals that participate in liquor production and trading, and other activities related to liquor production and trading within Vietnam's territory. This Circular is divided as follows: General Provisions (Chap. I); Liquor Production (Chap. II); Liquor Trading (Chap. III); Grant of Modified and Supplemented Licenses, Re-grant of Licenses for Liquor Production, Licenses for Small-Scale Production of Liquor for Sale, Certificates of Registration of Small-Scale Production of Liquor for Sale to Enterprises Licensed to Produce Liquor for Further Processing, and Licenses for Liquor Trading (Chap. IV); Reporting Regime (Chap. V); Implementation Provisions (Chap. VI).

Title: Circular No. 08/2013/TT-BYT guiding the advertisement of food under the management of the Ministry of Health.
Keywords: food safety and quality; processing/handling; certification; food additives; packaging/labelling; beverages; milk/dairy products; internal trade; international trade
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 14 articles divided into three Chapters, provides conditions, competence, dossiers and procedures for certifying contents of advertisements of food under the management of the Ministry of Health. This Circular applies to organizations and individuals engaged in the introduction and advertisement of food under the management of the Ministry Health in Vietnam's territory. The types of food subject to registration of advertisement are: 1. Functional food; 2. Micro-nutrient-fortified food; 3. Natural mineral water; 4. Bottled water; 5. Food additives, food processing aids; 6. Food packaging tools and food packages and containers used during food production, processing and trading. Prohibited acts in food advertising are: 1. Advertising food without written certifications of advertisement contents granted by competent health agencies. 2. Advertising counterfeit or poor-quality food. 3. Advertising food as having effects as medicines. 4. Advertising food not in conformity with the contents of the regulation conformity announcement or the announcement of conformity with food safety regulations or exaggerating the effects of food.

Title: Circular No. 11/2013/TT-BYT guiding the designation of regulation conformity certification organizations for prepackaged processed foods; food additives; food processing aids; and packaging and utensils in direct contact with food.
Keywords: food safety and quality; inspection; hygiene/sanitary procedures; packaging/labelling; food additives; registration; certification
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 9 articles divided into four Chapters, establishes the requirements and procedures for certifying foodstuff organizations. It specifies for prepackaged processed food; food additives; food processing aids; and packaging and utensils in direct contact with food subject to relevant technical regulations; responsibilities of receiving agencies and designated regulation conformity certification organizations. The Circular is divided as follows: General Provisions (Chap. I); Requirements, Order, Procedures for Registration and Designation of Regulation Conformity Certification Organizations (Chap. II); Responsibilities of the Receiving Agency and Designated Regulation Conformity Certification Organizations (Chap. III); Organization of Implementation (Chap. IV).

Title: Decision No. 1533/QD-TTg approving the master plan on socio-economic development of Can Tho city through 2020, with a vision toward 2030.
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Food & nutrition; Water
Keywords: agricultural development; policy/planning; agro-forestry; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; beverages; subsidy/incentive; fish products; processing/handling; inland waters; water supply; environmental planning; policy/planning
Abstract: This Decision approves the master plan on socio-economic development of Can Tho city through 2020, with a vision toward 2030, with the following principal contents: To restructure the city's economy toward industry-service-hi-tech agriculture through 2020 and service-industry-hi-tech agriculture after 2020; to focus on conditions to create posture and momentum for development (key infrastructure facilities, key sectors and products, technologies and human resources). The economic structure grows in a fast, stable and sustainable manner, ensuring social equity and progress. To invest in economic development in width and depth, mainly in depth. To focus investment on a number of the city's sectors with competitive advantages such as services, tourism, hi-tech industries; hi-tech agricultural and aquatic products; focusing on fields with comparative advantages for local development (such as: agro-forestry, fisheries processing industry, food-beverages, biotechnology, chemicals and biochemical products). To align the city's socio-economic development in the Mekong River Delta region; building and developing Can Tho city as a momentum for boosting the region's development. closely combine economic development with the development of social fields, education and training, healthcare, culture and building of a new countryside, gradually narrow the gap between urban and rural areas, etc. The master plan's overall objectives are: to build Can Tho city into a civilized and modem city imbued with river characteristics so as to basically become an industrialized city before 2020; a socio-economic center, an education-training and science-technology center, a healthcare, cultural and sports center in the Mekong River Delta region; an important hub of regional and international transport; a key area holding a strategic role in the Mekong River Delta region's and the country's defense and security; to play the role as a momentum to boost the strong development of the entire Mekong River Delta region.

Title: Decision No. 1831/QD-TTg approving the master plan on socio-economic development of Bac Ninh province through 2020, with orientations toward 2030.
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Food & nutrition; Water
Keywords: agricultural development; policy/planning; agro-forestry; capacity building; poverty; education; sustainable development; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; beverages; subsidy/incentive; fish products; processing/handling; capacity building; poverty; education; inland waters; water supply; environmental planning; capacity building; education; sustainable development; policy/planning
Abstract: This Decision approves the master plan on socio-economic development of Bac Ninh province through 2020, with orientations toward 2030, with the following principal contents: To bring into the fullest play the potential and advantages of its gateway position and human resources for fast and sustainable socio-economic development; to intensively develop highly competitive sectors; to focus investment on and attract human resources for the development of industries with strengths associated with goods production chains in the context of international economic integration; to strive to become a province with an important position in the Red River delta region’s economy. To combine economic development with realizing social progress and equity, gradually improve local people's life quality; to synchronously develop healthcare, culture, education and training; to implement hunger eradication and poverty reduction programs in a synchronous, comprehensive and effective manner, and build sustainable poverty reduction models; by 2020 there will be basically no poor households. To expand the scope and subjects involved in social insurance and unemployment insurance. The master plan's overall objectives are: to build Bac Ninh into a province with sustainable and harmonious development between urban and rural areas and a modern and synchronous infrastructure in which Bac Ninh urban center reach grade-I urban center standard; comprehensive healthcare, culture, education and training development, higher human resource quality and incessantly improved local people’s spiritual and material lives and assured eco-environment, political security, defense and social order and safety. By 2015, Bac Ninh will basically become a modernity-oriented industrial province and have all necessary technical infrastructural conditions for becoming a centrally run city in the 2020s.

Title: Decision No. 1291/QD-TTg approving the Plan of Action to develop the agricultural and fishery product processing industry in implementation of Vietnam's industrialization strategy within the framework of Vietnam-Japan cooperation through 2020, with a vision toward 2030.
Keywords: food safety and quality; processing/handling; public health; fish products; business/industry/corporations; institution; internal trade; good practices; governance
Abstract: This Decision approves the Plan of Action to develop the agricultural and fishery product processing industry in implementation of Vietnam's industrialization strategy within the framework of Vietnam-Japan cooperation through 2020, with a vision toward 2030. This Plan of Action, consisting of six sections, establishes necessary measures to work out solutions to increase the added value in each step of the supply chain. In order to participate in the global supply chain, solutions should be adopted and implemented in order to create a number of typical products and develop and raise the quality of agricultural, fishery and food products. The orientations toward 2020 aim to turn Vietnam into a reliable supplier of safe and high-quality agricultural, fishery and food products. The objectives by 2020 are: to develop the world market's trust in Vietnam as a producer of safe and high-quality agricultural, fishery and food products for export and domestic consumption and to create 3-5 processed agricultural, fishery and food commodity items which can help build Vietnamese brands and image. As strategic issues it aims at ensuring raw material sources with stable quality and quantity.

Title: Circular No. 34/2014/TT-BNNPTNT guiding the quality inspection of imported salt.
Keywords: condiments/herbs/spices; hygiene/sanitary procedures; internal trade; international trade; inspection; standards; food safety and quality
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 16 articles divided into four Chapters, provides the contents of and procedures for the quality inspection of imported salt within the state management of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development. The import of salt in the following cases is not regulated by this Circular but must comply with current management regulations: a) Temporary import for re-export, border-gate transfer or transit; b) Import for export production or goods processing for foreign traders; c/ Personal luggage, sample goods or goods for exhibitions or fairs, refined salts used as reagents in chemical analysis, salt in donations, and cosmetic salt. This Circular applies to domestic and foreign organizations and individuals that import salt and organizations and individuals involved in the quality inspection of imported salt. Grounds for inspection: 1. For imported food grade salt (table salt): National standard TCVN 3974-2007 on food grade salt. 2. For imported refined salt: National standard TCVN 9639-2013 on refined salt (NaCl). 3. For imported industrial salt: National standard TCVN9640-2013 on industrial salt (NaCl). 4. For imported salts specified in Clauses 1, 2 and 3: Criteria on heavy metals in salt provided in national technical regulation QCVN 8-2:2011/BYT on the limits of heavy metal contamination in food.

Title: Decree No. 100/2014/ND-CP on the trading in and use of nutritious products for infants, feeding bottles and teats.
Keywords: hygiene/sanitary procedures; internal trade; international trade; standards; food safety and quality; breastfeeding/infant food; nutrition; packaging/labelling
Abstract: This Decree, consisting of 16 articles divided into five Chapters, prescribes information, education, communication, advertising for, trading in and use of nutritious products for infants, feeding bottles and teats; responsibilities in trading in, and use of, nutritious products for infants so as to reduce the malnutrition rate based on the promotion and protection of breastfeeding - the best for the health and all-sided growth of infants.

Title: Circular No. 43/2014/TT-BYT providing for the management of functional food.
Keywords: food additives; nutrition; packaging/labelling; special dietary uses; food safety and quality; internal trade; international trade
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 21 articles divided into eight Chapters, provides for the activities related to the production, trading, announcement and labelling of, and provision of use instructions for, functional food, including supplemented food, health supplements/food supplements/dietary supplements and medical food (food for special medical purposes), and also food for special dietary uses. This Circular does not apply to infant formulas. The production, trading, announcement and labelling of, and provision of use instructions for, infant formulas must comply with relevant technical regulations and the provisions on trading and use of infant formulas. The Circular is divided as follows: General Provisions (Chap. I); General Requirements for Functional Food (Chap. II); Requirements on Supplemented Food (Chap. III); Requirements on Health Supplements (Chap. IV); Requirements on Medical Food and Food for Special Dietary Uses (Chap. V); Conditions for Production and Trading of, and Use Instructions for, Functional Food (Chap. VI); Recall and Disposal of Unsafe Functional Food (Chap. VII); Implementation Provisions (Chap. VIII).

Title: Ordinance No. 10/2014/UBTVQH13 on Environmental Police.
Keywords: institution; pollution control; inspection; ecosystem conservation; monitoring; access-to-information; governance; institution; inspection; food safety and quality; access-to-information; governance
Abstract: This Ordinance, consisting of 19 articles divided into five Chapters, provides the functions, duties, powers, organization, and assurance of operation of, and regimes and policies for, the Environmental Police; and prescribes responsibilities of related agencies, organizations and individuals. This Ordinance applies to the Environmental Police, Vietnamese agencies, organization and citizens and foreign organizations and individuals residing and operating in the territory of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam. The Environmental Police is a specialized People's Public Security force having the function of preventing, detecting, stopping and combating environmental crimes and environment-related administrative violations; and taking the initiative and coordinating with other forces in preventing and combating crimes and violations related to natural resources and environment-related food safety. The Environmental Police is entitled to: 1. collect information, analyze, assess and forecast the situation in order to give advice and recommendations to competent authorities in directing the prevention and combat of crimes and violations. 2. apply public security measures to prevent and combat crimes and violations. 3. receive, process and settle denunciations and reports on crimes and violations in accordance with law. 4. investigate crimes related to environment, natural resources and environment related food safety in accordance with law. 5. inspect the observance of law by agencies, organizations and individuals in accordance with law; to search vehicles, objects and places when directly detecting signs of crime or administrative violation or receiving denunciations or reports on crimes or administrative violations in accordance with national legislation.

Title: Decree No. 94/2012/ND-CP on wine production and trading.
Keywords: food safety and quality; inspection; hygiene/sanitary procedures; packaging/labelling; authorization/permit; certification; viticulture/oenological practices; internal trade; international trade; procedural matters; fraud; internal trade; packaging/labelling; investment
Abstract: This Decree, consisting of 32 articled divided into six Chapters, prescribes the production and sale of wine and drinking alcohol, including: investment, production, import, export, sale, and other activities related to the production and trading of wine and drinking alcohol (for producing wine are referred to as wine). This Decree is applicable to the organizations and individuals engaged in the production and trading of wine, and other activities relevant to the production and sale of wine in Vietnam’s territory. Foreign traders, foreign-invested enterprises in Vietnam governed by this Decree and other relevant legal documents. This Decree is divided as follows: General Provisions (Chap. I); Wine Production (Chap. II); Trading Wine (Chap. III); Violations and Handling Violations (Chap. IV); State Management Responsibility (Chap. V); Implementation Provisions (Chap. VI).

Title: Circular No. 60/2014/TT-BCT detailing a number of articles of the Government’s Decree No. 94/2012/ND-CP on liquor production and trading.
Keywords: food safety and quality; inspection; hygiene/sanitary procedures; packaging/labelling; authorization/permit; certification; viticulture/oenological practices; internal trade; international trade
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 18 articles divided into six Chapters, provides dossiers and procedures for grant, re-grant, modification, supplementation and revocation of industrial-scale liquor production licenses, licenses for small-scale production of liquor for sale, certificates of registration of small-scale production of liquor for sale to enterprises licensed to produce liquor for further processing, liquor product distribution licenses, liquor product wholesale licenses and liquor product retail licenses prescribed in the Government’s Decree No. 94/2012/ND-CP of 12 November 2012, on liquor production and trading. This Circular applies to domestic and foreign organizations and individuals engaged in liquor production and trading and other related activities within Vietnam’s territory. It does not apply to the on-premise sale of liquor (retail of drinks for immediate consumption). This Circular is divided as follows: General Provisions (Chap. I); Liquor Production (Chap. II); Liquor Trading (Chap. III); Grant of Modified and Supplemented Licenses, Re-grant of Licenses for Liquor Production, Licenses for Small-Scale Production of Liquor for Sale, Certificates of Registration of Small-Scale Production of Liquor for Sale to Enterprises Licensed to Produce Liquor for Further Processing, and Licenses for Liquor Trading (Chap. IV); Reporting Regime (Chap. V); Implementation Provisions (Chap. VI).

Title: Circular No. 54/2014/TT-BCT prescribing conditions for food safety assurance for processed milk production and trading establishments.
Keywords: food safety and quality; milk/dairy products; special dietary uses; processing/handling; internal trade; international trade; nutrition
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 13 articles divided into four Chapters, prescribes for food safety assurance conditions for processed milk production and trading establishments managed by the Ministry of Industry and Trade. This Circular applies to: a) Processed milk production and trading establishments; b) Competent state management agencies and organizations and individuals involved in the production and trading of processed milk. This Circular does not apply to: a) Small-scale processed milk production and trading establishments; bl Street catering service providers and street food vendors governed by the Ministry of Health's Circular No. 30/2012/TT-BYT of December 5, 2012, providing food safety conditions for street catering service providers and street food vendors. The Circular is divided as follows: General Provisions (Chap. I); Conditions for Food Safety Assurance for Production Establishments (Chap. II); Food Safety Assurance Conditions for Processed Milk-Trading Establishments (Chap. III); Implementation Provisions (Chap. IV).

Title: Circular No. 53/2014/TT-BCT prescribing conditions for food safety assurance for beer production and trading establishments.
Keywords: food safety and quality; special dietary uses; processing/handling; internal trade; international trade; beverages; nutrition
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 14 articles divided into four Chapters, prescribes for food safety requirements for beer production and trading establishments managed by the Ministry of Industry and Trade. This Circular applies to: a) Beer production and trading establishments; b) Competent state management agencies and organizations and individuals related to beer production and trading. This Circular does not apply to: a) Small-scale beer production and trading establishments; b) Street catering service providers and street food vendors governed by the Ministry of Health's Circular No. 30/2012/TT-BYT of 5 December 2012, providing food safety conditions for street catering service providers and street food vendors. The Circular is divided as follows: General Provisions (Chap. I); Conditions for Food Safety Assurance for Beer Production Establishments (Chap. II); Food Safety Assurance Conditions for Beer Trading Establishments (Chap. III); Implementation Provisions (Chap. IV).

Title: Circular No. 29/2012/TT-BCT providing the grant and revocation of certificates of food safety eligibility for establishments managed by the Ministry of Industry and Trade.
Keywords: food safety and quality; certification; legal proceedings/administrative proceedings
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 13 articles divided into five Chapters, provides the grant and revocation of certificates of food safety eligibility for establishments managed by the Ministry of Industry and Trade. It provides dossiers, procedures and competence to grant and revoke certificates of food safety eligibility for food production and trading establishments managed by the Ministry of Industry and Trade; and general food trading establishments. This Circular applies to: a) Food production and trading establishments managed by the Ministry of Industry and Trade, including establishments producing or trading in liquor, beer, beverages, processed milk, vegetable oil, products processed from flour and starch, and confectionery, and integral packaging tools and materials that are exclusively used for the above food products. b) General food trading establishments, excluding wholesale marketplaces and farm-produce auction marketplaces. This Circular does not apply to: a) Small-scale food production and trading establishments; b) Street vendors; c) Establishments trading in prepackaged food which do not require special preservation conditions under regulations.

Title: Circular No. 53/2014/TT-BCT prescribing conditions for food safety assurance for vegetable oil production and trading establishments.
Keywords: food safety and quality; special dietary uses; processing/handling; internal trade; international trade; oils/oilseeds/fats; agricultural commodities; nutrition
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 13 articles divided into four Chapters, This Circular prescribes food safety requirements for vegetable oil production and trading establishments managed by the Ministry of Industry and Trade. This Circular applies to: a) Vegetable oil production and trading establishments; b) Competent state management agencies and organizations and individuals involved in vegetable oil production and trading. It does not apply to: a) Small-scale vegetable oil production and trading establishments; b) Street catering service providers and street food vendors governed by the Ministry of Health's Circular No. 30/2012/TT-BYT of 5 December 2012, providing food safety conditions for street catering service providers and street food vendors.. The Circular is divided as follows: General Provisions (Chap. I); Conditions for Food Safety Assurance for Vegetable Oil Production Establishments (Chap. II); Food Safety Assurance Conditions for Vegetable Oil Trading Establishments (Chap. III); Implementation Provisions (Chap. IV).

Title: Circular No. 14/2015/TT-BTC guiding the classification, analysis for classification; analysis for quality inspection and food safety inspection of exports and imports.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection; internal trade; international trade; certification; classification/declassification
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 16 articles, guides the classification, use of analysis results for classification and use of analysis results for quality inspection and food safety inspection of imports and exports. Customs declarants, customs offices, customs officers and other organizations and individuals, when performing jobs related to classification, quality inspection and food safety inspection of exports and imports, shall comply with this Circular. The Circular specifies food hygiene and safety inspection procedures for export from and import to Viet Nam.

Title: Circular No. 12/2015/TT-BNNPTNT guiding food safety inspection of imports of plant origin.
Keywords: food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection; internal trade; international trade; classification/declassification; vegetables/legumes; registration
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 29 articles divided into eight Chapters, prescribes the method of inspection, the order of and procedures for food safety registration and inspection of goods of plant origin used as food which are imported into Vietnam under the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development; and responsibilities and powers of related parties. The goods governed by this Circular are food of plant origin defined in Appendix 2 to Joint Circular No. 13/2014/TTLT-BYT-BNNPTNT-BCT of 9 April 2014, guiding the assignment and coordination in state management of food safety. This Circular does not govern contents and provisions on plant quarantine activities. It applies to domestic and foreign organizations and individuals engaged in the production of and trading in goods of plant origin used as food which are imported into Vietnam under the management of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development.

Title: Circular No. 09/2015/TT-BYT providing for the certification of advertising contents for special products, goods and services under the management of the Ministry of Health.
Keywords: certification; food safety and quality; packaging/labelling; mineral water; food additives; standards; good practices
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 27 articles divided into three Chapters, provides for the conditions, competence, dossiers and procedures for certification of advertising contents for special products, goods and services under the management of the Ministry of Health, including: 1. Drugs not banned from advertising as prescribed in Clause 5, Article 7 of the Law on Advertising. 2. Cosmetics. 3. Food and food additives under the management of the Ministry of Health, including: a) Functional food; b) Food fortified with micro-nutrients; c) Natural mineral water; d) Bottled drinking water; dd) Food additives and food processing aids. 4. Chemicals, insecticides and disinfectants for household and medical use. 5. Medical equipment. 6. Milk and nutritious products for children under the management of the Ministry of Health which are not banned from advertising as prescribed in Clause 4, Article 7 of the Law on Advertising. 7. Medical examination and treatment services. This Circular applies to organizations and individuals involved in the certification of advertising contents for special products, goods and services under the management of the Ministry of Health in the territory of Vietnam. Chapter II deals with: Conditions, Competence, Dossiers and Procedures for Certification of Advertising Contents for Special Products, Goods and Services under the Management of the Ministry of Health.

Title: Decision No. 1467/QD-TTg approving the Scheme on development of regional markets in the 2015-2020 period, with a vision toward 2030.
Keywords: agricultural development; internal trade; international trade; coffee/cocoa/tea; policy/planning; cereals/grains/rice; agricultural commodities; crops ; vegetables/legumes; cereals/grains/rice; aquatic animals; fruits/edible nuts; policy/planning; food safety and quality; fraud; internal trade; agricultural commodities
Abstract: This Decision approves the Scheme on development of regional markets in the 2015-2020 period, with orientations toward 2030 with the following principal contents: Viewpoints on development of regional markets are: a) To promote and diversify Vietnam’s export markets in order to boost domestic production, create employment and solve social issues; at the same time, to increase foreign-currency revenues for the country, and raise the level of technology to serve national industrialization and modernization; b) To develop export in the direction of sustainable growth and suitability to characteristics of each regional market, ensuring both expansion and increase of added value of export; c) To develop export markets in line with the restructuring of exported goods toward industrialization and modernization on the basis of the 2011-2020 socio-economic development strategy and the strategy on import and export of goods in the 2011-2020 period, with orientations toward 2030; d) To orient and boost export according to regional markets: Southeast Asia, Northeast Asia, Chinese-speaking market, Oceania, Europe (European Union, Central and Eastern Europe, Russia and CIS countries), the United States and Canada, Latin America, Africa, West Asia, and South Asia. Its general objectives are: To strive to increase the export value to USD 300 billion by 2020, and an average annual export growth rate of 11%-12% from 2015 to 2020; to strive to achieve a stable trade balance by 2020 and sustainable trade surplus in subsequent years. Specific targets establish the characteristics of and export situation in each regional market, the specific export targets by 2020 and 2030 for each regional market. It aims to step up the export of industrial processed and manufactured goods with high technological content and input materials; to continue to promote and maintain the export of agricultural, forest and aquatic goods, especially Vietnam’s advantageous ones like rice, foodstuffs, vegetables and fruits, coffee, cocoa, rubber products, and aquatic products.

Title: National Action Plan on Combating Drug Resistance in the period of 2013–2020.
Keywords: animal health; pests/diseases; inspection; hygiene/sanitary procedures; data collection/reporting; agricultural development; drugs; capacity building; public health; antimicrobial resistance (AMR); food safety and quality; capacity building; public health; fish products
Abstract: This National Action Plan is a sectoral policy document. The National Action Plan aims to cope with the challenge of antimicrobial resistance, improve the management of veterinary antimicrobial agents, ensure the safety of animal production, food safety, public health and ecological security, and maintain the people's health. Main goals include: to significantly improve veterinary antimicrobial resistance, the capacity on antimicrobial drug residue management, the level of standardized drug use in animal production and aquaculture sectors, and quality and safety level of animal, poultry and aquatic products. Main actions are: to promote to gradually withdraw antimicrobials used as animal growth promoters, in particular to strengthen the risk assessment of main veterinary medicines and early warning; to strengthen the supervision of veterinary antimicrobials, including market access, drug use in animal production and aquaculture and medicated feed; to improve the veterinary antimicrobial resistance monitoring system and network; to strengthen the supervision and control of veterinary antimicrobial residue, including: to implement the assessment of veterinary antimicrobial-caused environmental hazards, to carry out the detection of antimicrobial residues in livestock manure, to establish evaluation methods and standards.

Title: Plan for people’s health protection, care and promotion 2016-2020.
Keywords: public health; antimicrobial resistance (AMR); drugs; public health; sustainable development; data collection/reporting; governance; early warning system; risk assessment/management; climate change; food safety and quality; public health; poverty
Abstract: The overall objectives of the Plan for people’s health protection, care and promotion 2016-2020 aims: to reduce mortality rates caused by diseases and epidemics; to contribute to improving people’s life expectancy and core health indicators; to enhance performance of the health system, to create a solid foundation for the development of an equitable, effective, quality and sustainable health system. Its specific objectives are: implement universal health coverage (UHC), ensuring access to basic and quality health care services; prevent occurrence of major epidemics; to gradually control health hazards related to the environment, food safety, lifestyle and behaviors of people; improve quality and effectiveness of service delivery network; ensure collaboration, linkage and integration among levels of care, among health care services (PHC); reduce overcrowding in hospitals; promote the leading roles of intensive health facilities in the transfer of techniques and support to the entire service delivery network; modernize and develop traditional medicine, and combine traditional medicine with modern medicine; maintain a reasonably low fertility rate; meet the needs for family planning services of the people; increase access to quality reproductive health care services; develop health care in poor, mountainous, remote areas and to have preferential treatment in health care for poor people and beneficiaries of social policies, etc. The food safety management system has been established from the Central to the local level, and the food safety inspection system includes National Food Safety and Hygiene Inspection Institute, regional inspection centers, State inspection authorities for imported food, basically meeting demand for food safety inspection. Communication and education on food safety on mass media have been promoted. Regarding socio-economic factors, the difference in per capita income between regions and population groups is big; the poverty rate remains high, especially in ethnic minority areas, along with many backward practices leading to unequal health, the burden of disease and limited access to health care services. As for natural environmental factors, Vietnam is one of the six countries bearing the greatest impact of climate change and is one of the five countries with the highest disaster risks in the world. Environmental pollution becomes increasingly serious, especially due to the fast urbanization. Food contamination due to the use of banned chemicals in aquaculture and food processing remains a persistent problem that has not been effectively controlled.

Title: Circular No. 21/2018/TT-BNNPTNT regulating the recording, submission of reports and logbooks of aquatic resources; publication of designated fishing ports confirming the origin of fisheries from exploitation; list of illegal fishing vessels; certification of raw materials, certification of exploited aquatic resources issued by the Minister of Agriculture and Rural Development.
Keywords: certification; authorization/permit; procedural matters; certification; authorization/permit; agricultural development; aquatic animals; procedural matters; aquaculture; harbour; port State measures; fishery management and conservation; marine fisheries; foreign fishing; Illegal unreported and unregulated fishing (IUU fishing); enforcement/compliance; marine fishes; molluscs; certification; food safety and quality; aquatic animals
Abstract: This Circular, consisting of 21 articles divided into six Chapters and seven Appendixes, prescribes the regulating the recording, submission of reports and logbooks of aquatic resources; publication of designated fishing ports confirming the origin of fisheries from exploitation; list of illegal fishing vessels; certification of raw materials, certification of exploited aquatic resources issued by the Minister of Agriculture and Rural Development as well as processing and export to the market. Logs and reports on fishing operations for fishing vessels must contain the following information: aquatic resources caught by fishing vessels up to 12 meters, record the fishing log according to the Form No. 01 Appendix I; logbooks manually submit medical products for the fishing ports management organization within 24 hours after completion of the landing operations in the fishery port. Trawling vessels from 6 to 12 meters record fishing report under M03 Appendix I; submit the fishing exploitation report to the fishing port management organization periodically once a week. Chapter 3 specifies the Fisheries Port Indicators to Certify the Origin of Aquatic Seafood. Article 6 specifies a list of designated fishing ports with sufficient system to confirm the origin of aquatic products meeting the criteria of fishing port of class I or class II. Article 7 deals with the inspection of fishing vessels and monitoring of fishery production at fishing ports; Article 8 lists illegal fishing vessels. Chapter 4 deals with Certification of Raw Materials Original Source; Chapters 5 and 6 deal with the Responsibilities and Powers of Organizations and Individuals; Organization and Implementation.

Title: Intended Nationally Determined Contributions of Viet Nam.
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Air & atmosphere; Environment; Food & nutrition; Forestry; Land & soil; Water
Keywords: climate change; agricultural development; sustainable development; sustainable use; early warning system; food security; poverty; climate change; hazards; environmental planning; emissions; desertification; early warning system; risk assessment/management; sustainable development; soil conservation/soil improvement; governance; ecosystem conservation; biodiversity; tourism; poverty; policy/planning; food security; climate change; forest management/forest conservation; hazards; ecosystem conservation; risk assessment/management; poverty; climate change; hazards; desertification; soil conservation/soil improvement; poverty; climate change; water resources management; hazards; risk assessment/management; sustainable development; climate change; emissions; environmental planning; policy/planning
Abstract: Viet Nam is one of the first countries to ratify the UNFCCC and the KP and has actively been researching and implementing GHG mitigation measures. As of June 2015, Viet Nam had 254 Clean Development Mechanism (CDM) projects accredited and registered by the CDM Executive Board (EB). Viet Nam is ranked number four internationally for number of projects, with a total GHG reduction amount of approximately 137.4 million tCO2e in the credit period. Among the 254 projects, energy projects account for 87.6%, waste treatment for 10.2%, reforestation and afforestation for 0.4% and other projects for 1.8%. To date, more than 12 million Certified Emission Reductions (CERs) credits have been issued by the EB for Viet Nam, which is ranked eleventh in the world. The Government has issued many policies on energy saving and efficiency, such as the “National Target Programme on Energy Efficiency” (2006), the Law on “Economical and Efficient use of Energy” (2010). The Government has prioritized policies, such as renewable energy development, consistent with Viet Nam’s mitigation potential and conditions, in order to contribute to energy security and environmental protection. Policies encouraging energy savings and efficiencies in production and daily life, through the application of energy saving and renewable energy technologies, are also a priority. Viet Nam has made significant efforts in forest protection, afforestation and reforestation, and is one of the countries participating in Reducing Emissions from Deforestation and Forest Degradation, sustainable management of forests, conservation of forest carbon stocks and enhancement of forest carbon stocks and enhancement of forest carbon stocks (REDD+). Viet Nam is developing and preparing for the implementation of Nationally Appropriate Mitigation Actions (NAMAs), as well as the registration and implementation of carbon credit projects according to the Verified Carbon Standard (VCS) and the Gold Standard (GS) (pags. 2 and 3). In order to achieve the mitigation targets, Viet Nam will exert efforts in implementing the following measures: 1) Strengthen the leading role of the State in responding to climate change; 2) Improve effectiveness and efficiency of energy use; reducing energy consumption; 3) Change the fuel structure in industry and transportation; 4) Promote effective exploitation and increase the proportion of new and renewable energy sources in energy production and consumption; 5) Reduce GHG emissions through the development of sustainable agriculture; improve effectiveness and competitiveness of agricultural production; 6) Manage and develop sustainable forest, enhance carbon sequestration and environmental services; conservation of biodiversity associated with livelihood development and income generation for communities and forest-dependent people (pags. 5 and 6). Viet Nam is one of the countries severely affected by climate change and its related disasters. The Mekong Delta is one of the deltas in the world most susceptible and vulnerable to sea level rise. Climate change adaptation is vital for Viet Nam and is regarded by the Government as one of the priority tasks to reduce the vulnerability level. The INDC is an official and important channel for conveying information and experience in adaptation, including sharing risks and damages, to the international community. The National Climate Change Strategy has identified that priorities are food security, energy security, water security, poverty reduction, gender equality, social security, public health, livelihood improvements and the protection of natural resources. These goals can only be achieved through enhancing the adaptive capacity of human and socio-economic systems as well as natural systems. Through its INDC, Viet Nam can communicate its current and future climate change response efforts implemented with national resources, and what can be done better with additional international support (pags. 7 and 8).

Title: Scheme for Restructuring of Vietnam’s Rice Industry by 2025 and 2030.
Keywords: agricultural commodities; food safety and quality; food security; capacity building; cereals/grains/rice; climate change; hazards; ecological production/organic production; gender; good practices; processing/handling; innovation; internal trade; international trade; policy/planning; post-harvest treatment; risk assessment/management; agricultural commodities; agricultural development; breeders' rights; capacity building; cereals/grains/rice; climate change; hazards; ecological production/organic production; gender; good practices; processing/handling; innovation; internal trade; international trade; policy/planning; plant variety; post-harvest treatment; protection of environment; risk assessment/management; crops ; intellectual property rights
Abstract: This Scheme for Restructuring of Vietnam’s Rice Industry by 2025 and 2030 is a sectoral policy document covering the period of 2021-2030. The policy document calls for continuing to restructure the rice industry by improving productivity and facilitating sustainable development with the following general objectives: (i) meet domestic demand, act as the core of national food security, (ii) improve quality and nutritional value, ensure food safety, (iii) establish and improve the rice chain value, (iv) adapt to and mitigate climate change, (v) utilize natural resources and protect the environment, (vi) increase farmer income and consumer benefits, and (vii) export high-quality and high-value rice. Specific objectives by 2025 are set up in the policy document.  Objectives by 2025 include: 1 Maintain paddy field area at 3,6-3,7 million ha, planting area at 7,0-7,2 million ha and rice yield at 40-41 million tonnes. 2. Export approximately 5 million tonnes of rice with fragrant rice, specialty rice and japonica rice accounting for 40%, glutinous rice 20%, high-quality milled rice 20%,  medium- and low-quality rice 15% and rice products 5%; branded rice accounts for more than 20% of exported rice. 3. Use certified seeds on more than 80% and high-quality seeds on more than 70% of planting area; reduce direct seeding (80 kg/ha on average) by 70%; apply advanced cultivation processes  and good production practices (VietGAP and equivalent, climate-smart rice cultivation, organic agriculture, etc.) on more than 60% and high technology and digital technology on 10% of planting area. 4. Reduce use of chemical fertilizers and agrochemicals in rice production by at least 30%. 5. Keep post-harvest loss under 8%. 6. Rice production automation reaches 70% on average and more than 90% in the Mekong Delta. 7. Increase planting area production upon which is connected with trade to more than 30%. 7. More than 30% of profit go to farmers. 8. Reduce greenhouse gas emission in rice production to 5%. Objectives by 2030 include: 1. Maintain paddy field area at 3,5 million ha, use planting area flexibly and ensure annual rice yield of at least 35 million tonnes. 2. Export approximately 4 million tonnes of rice with fragrant rice, specialty rice and japonica rice accounting for 45%, glutinous rice 20%, high-quality milled rice 15%, medium- and low-quality rice 10% and rice products 10%; branded rice accounts for more than 40% of exported rice. 3. Use certified seeds on more than 90% and high-quality seeds on more than 80% of planting area; reduce direct seeding (80 kg/ha on average) by 80%; apply advanced cultivation processes and good production practices (VietGAP and equivalent, climate-smart rice cultivation, organic agriculture, etc.) on more than 70% and high technology and digital technology on 20% of planting area. 4. Reduce use of chemical fertilizers and agrochemicals in rice production by at least 40%. 5. Keep post-harvest loss under 5%. 6. Rice production automation reaches 80% on average and more than 100% in the Mekong Delta. 7. Increase planting area production upon which is connected with trade to more than 50%. 8. More than 30% of profit go to farmers. - Reduce greenhouse gas emission in rice production to 10%. To achieve the objectives, the Scheme for Restructuring of Vietnam’s Rice Industry by 2025 and 2030 further proposes a set of solutions, covering the fields of: rice variety development (variety structure orientations and variety production and trade), application of good production practices, post-harvest, storage and processing, quality control and food safety, production reform, market development, enhancement of adaptability to climate change, adverse conditions and risks, efficient use of natural resources, environmental protection and preservation of rice-related cultural values, human resource developmentetc,  gender issues in rice production, etc.

Title: Circular No.: 26/2016/TT-BNNPTNT providing for the Quarantine of Aquatic Animals And Animal Products
Keywords: animal health; aquaculture; aquatic animals; fish diseases; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection; molluscs; monitoring; quarantine; residues; transport/storage; drugs; international trade; trade in species; fish products; food safety and quality; residues; risk assessment/management; standards; toxicity/poisoning; traceability; transport/storage; drugs
Abstract: This Circular stipulates in detail Paragraph 3, Article 53 of the Law on Animal Health on the List of aquatic animals and animal products subject to the quarantine and non-quarantine; the List of aquatic animals and animal products subject to the analysis of risks before imported into Vietnam; the List of subjects performing the quarantine of aquatic animals and animal products; the content and dossier on quarantine of aquatic animals and animal products transported out of the provincial areas, for export, import, temporary import for re-export, temporary export for re-import, border-gate transfer, bonded warehouse transfer, transit in the territory of Vietnam. This Circular applies to the organizations and individuals pertaining to the production, trading and transportation out of the provincial areas; for export, import, temporary import for re-export, temporary export for re-import, border-gate transfer, bonded warehouse transfer and transit in the territory of Vietnam.

Title: Circular No.: 25/2016/TT-BNNPTNT providing for the Quarantine of Terrestial Animals And Animal Products
Keywords: animal feed/feedstuffs; animal health; authorization/permit; enforcement/compliance; data collection/reporting; pests/diseases; hygiene/sanitary procedures; transport/storage; inspection; international trade; monitoring; residues; standards; vaccination; drugs; fish products; food safety and quality; residues; risk assessment/management; standards; toxicity/poisoning; traceability; transport/storage; drugs
Abstract: This Circular guides the provisions in Paragraph 3, Article 37 of the Law on Animal Health as follows: a) The List of terrestrial animals and animal products subject to the quarantine; the List of terrestrial animals and animal products subject to non-quarantine; the List of terrestrial animals and animal products subject to the analysis of risks before imported into Vietnam; the List of subjects of quarantine of terrestrial animals and animal products; b) The content and dossier on quarantine of terrestrial animals and animal products transported out of the provincial areas; for export, import, temporary import for re-export, temporary export for re-import, border-gate transfer, bonded warehouse transfer, transit in the territory of Vietnam and terrestrial animals and animal products carried along; marking and issue of code of terrestrial animals, seal of means of transportation and containers of terrestrial animals and animal products subject to the quarantine. This Circular applies to the organizations and individuals pertaining to the production, trading and transportation out of the provincial areas; export, import, temporary import for re-export, temporary export for re-import, border-gate transfer, bonded warehouse transfer, transit in the territory of Vietnam of the terrestrial animals and animal products.

Title: Circular No.: 09/2016/TT-BNNPTNT providing for Animal Slaughter Control And Veterinary Hygiene Inspection
Keywords: animal health; classification/declassification; enforcement/compliance; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection; pests/diseases; slaughtering; good practices; standards; certification; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection; internal trade; international trade; packaging/labelling; meat; poultry products; slaughtering; waste management
Abstract: This Circular details the implementation of clauses 2 and 3 Article 74 of the Law on veterinary medicine. To be specific: 1. List of animals subject to slaughter control; List of entities subject to veterinary hygiene inspection; List of subjects of veterinary hygiene inspection. 2. Procedures for animal slaughter control; procedures and documents for veterinary hygiene inspection; samples of stamps of slaughter control and stamps of veterinary hygiene; regulations on the handling of animals/animal products unconformable to veterinary hygiene requirements. This Circular applies to domestic and foreign agencies, organizations and individuals relevant to the slaughter of terrestrial animals, the preparation and process of animals and animal products and the inspection of veterinary hygiene within Vietnam’s territory.

Title: Plan to Promote Investment in the Agricultural and Rural Sector in the Period of 2021-25 (Decision No. 5227/2020/QD-BNN-KH).
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Livestock; Food & nutrition; Fisheries; Forestry; Cultivated plants
Keywords: investment; agricultural development; protection of environment; food security; afforestation/reforestation; agricultural commodities; aquaculture; ecological production/organic production; emissions; innovation; irrigation; monitoring; research; sustainable development; climate change; processing/handling; internal trade; international trade; fertilizers/nutrients; investment; animal feed/feedstuffs; animal production; emissions; innovation; climate change; processing/handling; internal trade; international trade; investment; climate change; sustainable use; innovation; integrated management; policy/planning; aquaculture; food safety and quality; food security; processing/handling; innovation; internal trade; international trade; investment; climate change; sustainable development; sustainable use; afforestation/reforestation; forest management/forest conservation; investment; agricultural development; protection of environment; agricultural commodities; ecological production/organic production; emissions; innovation; monitoring; research; sustainable development; climate change; processing/handling; internal trade; international trade; fertilizers/nutrients; plant production
Abstract: This Plan to Promote Investment in the Agricultural and Rural Sector in the Period of 2021-25 is a plan at national level. The common goal of the Plan include: Formulate an investment promotion plan in agriculture and rural areas for the period of 2021-2025 in order to improve the efficiency and uniformity in the implementation of investment promotion activities of the sector for each year; ensure the successful implementation of the sector development goals and in line with the orientation of attracting investment in priority and investment promotion contents of each field of agriculture, forestry, fishery and rural development. The Plan sets up the following specific goals: 1. Strengthen the implementation of investment promotion activities to effectively implement the Plan to restructure the agricultural sector towards sustainable agricultural development, improve added value, efficiency and competitiveness of agricultural products. 2. To step up the attraction of investment in the development of modern agriculture, clean agriculture, organic agriculture, adaptation to climate change and investment in the development of agricultural mechanization and agro-processing industry. 3. Create a favorable and equal competitive environment for investment in agriculture and rural areas; attracting over 18,000 enterprises to directly invest in agriculture and rural areas. 4. Promote the attraction of foreign direct investment projects and investment projects of large corporations in order to create a driving force for the development of agricultural production in the direction of commodity production, connecting with the supply chain. The priority areas for investment attraction are proposed in the Plan,  including: 1. Strengthen investment promotion in the development of high-tech application of agriculture, clean agriculture, and organic agriculture to ensure food safety, environmental friendliness and efficient use of natural resources; 2. Prioritize attracting investment projects to promote agricultural mechanization and processing and preservation of key agricultural products, aiming to improve agricultural production efficiency and reduce post-harvest losses, and diversifying processed agricultural products, meeting the standards and regulations on quality management of the consumer market; 3. Attract investment in the field of science and technology transfer and application; promote the application of high technology, digital technology and information technology in all stages of the value chain, especially in collecting information, forecasting the situation of the agricultural product consumption market, timely supplying market information and services for businesses, cooperatives and farmers to proactively organize production plans according to market demand; 4. To encourage investment in developing increasingly modern agricultural and rural infrastructure and commercial infrastructure in the countryside; 5. Promote investment promotion in the development of the supply and distribution system of agricultural products, build a wholesale market / modern agricultural product supply center to meet domestic and international standards, an auction center, E-commerce trading floor to introduce, promote and auction agricultural products; 6. Attract investment in developing tourism services associated with agriculture and rural areas, community tourism associated with traditional industries and developing local specialties; 7. Encourage insurance enterprises to participate in agricultural insurance to reduce risks in agricultural production, towards sustainable production.

Title: Resolution No. 53/NQCP on measures to encourage and facilitate businesses to invest in agriculture in an effective, safe and sustainable manner.
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Livestock; Food & nutrition; Fisheries; Forestry; Cultivated plants
Keywords: investment; agricultural development; food security; afforestation/reforestation; agricultural commodities; ecological production/organic production; innovation; monitoring; research; sustainable development; climate change; processing/handling; internal trade; international trade; capacity building; financial agricultural measures; fiscal and market measures; investment; animal feed/feedstuffs; animal production; innovation; processing/handling; internal trade; international trade; capacity building; investment; sustainable use; innovation; integrated management; policy/planning; aquaculture; capacity building; food safety and quality; food security; processing/handling; innovation; internal trade; international trade; investment; sustainable development; sustainable use; afforestation/reforestation; forest management/forest conservation; capacity building; investment; agricultural development; protection of environment; agricultural commodities; ecological production/organic production; innovation; research; sustainable development; processing/handling; internal trade; international trade; capacity building
Abstract: This Resolution No. 53/NQCP on Measures to Encourage and Facilitate Businesses to Invest in Agriculture in an Effective, Safe and Sustainable Manner is a policy document at national covering the period of 2019-2030. Vision to 2030 is set up as: 1. To develop agriculture in the direction of modernity and sustainability; produce large goods, apply science and technology, innovate to increase productivity, quality, efficiency and competitiveness; improve and increasingly raise the living standards of farmers; modern civilized rural construction. 2. By 2030, Vietnam's agriculture will be among the 15 most developed countries in the world, in which the agricultural product processing industry will be among the top 10 countries in the world. Vietnam is a deep processing center of world agriculture, a logistics center of global agricultural trade. 3. Agricultural enterprises are identified as having the role of pillars in promoting the development of agricultural production in our country towards commodity production, improving the competitiveness of Vietnamese agricultural products. To achieve the Vision to 2030, the policy document sets up the following specific targets by 2030: 1. The growth rate of agricultural, forestry and fishery production value is about 3.0%/year. 2. The growth rate of export turnover of agriculture, forestry and fishery will reach about 6%-8%/year. 3. By 2030, there will be 80,000 to 100,000 enterprises with effective business investment activities in the agricultural sector, of which about 3,000 to 4,000 are large-scale enterprises and 6,000 to 8,000 medium-sized enterprises. The main tasks are proposed in the policy document, including: 1. Continue to perfect institutions and reform administrative procedures; sharply cut barriers on business conditions in agriculture, creating attractiveness for enterprises to invest in agriculture; 2. To develop production, processing and consumption of agricultural products in conformity with market demands, plans and strategies for the development of the agricultural sector; 3. Renovate the mechanism to support enterprises in market expansion promotion, step by step take the initiative in the market; 4. Improve credit policies for agricultural projects in the direction of supporting and encouraging investment in the agricultural sector; 5. The Ministry of Finance shall assume the prime responsibility for, and coordinate with ministries, branches and localities in, studying, proposing and adjusting tax policies reasonably in order to encourage enterprises to invest in agriculture and ensure the stability of macro policies; 6. Formulate and perfect policies to create favorable conditions for enterprises to access and apply scientific research results and modern advanced technologies to serve production and business in agriculture; make the most of the opportunities of the 4.0 industrial revolution for outstanding, efficient and sustainable agricultural development; 7. To perfect the operating mechanism for the land use right market, to create a synchronous mechanism for the development of the agricultural land use right market; urgently study a pilot mechanism on land accumulation and concentration, ensuring the harmony of interests of the State, farmers and enterprises; 8. Develop and improve the quality of human resources to serve production and business activities of enterprises in agriculture; etc.

Title: Scheme for Developing Organic Agriculture in the Period 2020-2030 (Decision No. 885/2020/QD-TTg).
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Livestock; Food & nutrition; Fisheries; Forestry; Cultivated plants
