Keywords: ecological production/organic production; agricultural development; agricultural commodities; aquaculture; innovation; irrigation; monitoring; research; sustainable development; processing/handling; internal trade; international trade; fertilizers/nutrients; certification; traceability; ecological production/organic production; animal feed/feedstuffs; animal production; emissions; innovation; processing/handling; internal trade; international trade; certification; drugs; animal health; traceability; ecological production/organic production; sustainable use; innovation; integrated management; policy/planning; aquaculture; certification; traceability; food safety and quality; processing/handling; innovation; internal trade; international trade; certification; traceability; ecological production/organic production; sustainable development; sustainable use; afforestation/reforestation; forest management/forest conservation; certification; traceability; ecological production/organic production; agricultural development; protection of environment; agricultural commodities; innovation; monitoring; research; sustainable development; processing/handling; internal trade; international trade; fertilizers/nutrients; certification; plant production
Abstract: This Scheme for Developing Organic Agriculture in the Period 2020-2030 is a policy document at national level.  The policy document sets up the following overall goal: Develop organic agriculture with high added value, sustainable, eco-friendly, associated with circular agricultural economy for domestic consumption and export. Certified organic agricultural products in accordance with regional and international organic agricultural standards; making Vietnam become a country with the level of organic agricultural production on par with advanced countries in the world. The Plan sets up the following specific goals to be achieved by 2030: 1. The area of ​​agricultural land group for organic production is about 2.5 - 3% of the total area of ​​agricultural land group. 2. The area of ​​organic farming land is about 2% of the total arable land with the main crops: rice, vegetables of all kinds, fruit trees, tea, pepper, coffee, cashew, coconut. 3. The percentage of organic livestock products is about 2-3% of the total domestic production of livestock products. Certified organic livestock products include: Milk, honey products, bird's nest products, poultry meat. 4. The area of ​​organic aquaculture will reach about 1.5-3% of the total area of ​​aquaculture, in which a number of special aquatic species have economic value: brackish water shrimp, giant freshwater shrimp, aquatic species local produce. 5. The production area of ​​organic nutrient salts accounts for over 10% of the total nutritional salt production area. 6. For medicinal and non-timber forest products from nature, the ratio of organic production to total output is about 95 - 98%, for intensive farming (using the forest environment for production) the rate is about 95 - 98%. The ratio of organic production to total production is about 80-85%. - Product value per hectare of organic farming and aquaculture land is 1.5 - 1.8 times higher than that of non-organic production. The main tasks and solutions are proposed in the Plan,  including: 1. Develop concentrated organic agricultural production areas by building a distribution system along the value chain from production and processing in association with the organic product consumption market, identifing areas with potential strengths in the production of organic agricultural products to encourage businesses, cooperatives and people who have demand to produce organic agricultural products to invest in production; 2. Management of certified organic plant, animal and aquatic product varieties in accordance with organic agricultural standards; 3. Strictly manage input materials used in organic production: fertilizers, pesticides, veterinary drugs, preservatives, additives; 4. Manage organic farming processes, organic livestock and aquatic product care processes; 5. Establish a traceability system for organic products at each stage of production, processing and consumption;  6. Improve institutions, mechanisms and policies for the development of organic agriculture; 7. Promote scientific research and technology transfer, international cooperation; etc.

Title: National Aquaculture Development Program for the Period of 2021 - 2030.
Keywords: animal reproduction; aquaculture; aquatic animals; business/industry/corporations; capacity building; climate change; fish diseases; fish products; freshwater fishes; innovation; internal trade; international trade; investment; mariculture; animal feed/feedstuffs; animal health; marine fishes; molluscs; policy/planning; protection of environment; research; sustainable development; aquatic plants; ecofriendly products/ecofriendly processes; ecological production/organic production; aquatic animals; fish products; food safety and quality; traceability; internal trade; international trade
Abstract: This National Aquaculture Development Program for the Period of 2021 - 2030 is a sectoral policy document. The general objectives are set up as: Develop aquaculture in a manner that is effective, sustainable, and adapting to climate change; improve efficiency, quality, value, and competitiveness of aquaculture products; satisfy domestic and export market requirements. By 2030, aquaculture productivity reaches 7 million tonne/year, creates jobs, and increases income for employees. To achieve the above objectives, specific objectives are identified in the document. For the period of 2021 - 2025, the specific objectives include: 1. By 2025, total aquaculture productivity reaches 5,6 million tonne/year, export turnover reaches 7,8 billion USD/year, aquaculture value growth rate reaches 4,0 %/year on average. 2. Actively produce and supply more than 50% of giant tiger prawns parents, more than 25% of whiteleg shrimps parents, and more than 70% of selectively bred pangasius fish parents; actively produce and supply sufficient breeders of high economic value and great commodity quantity. 3. Invest, upgrade infrastructures essential for production of more than 30 centralized aquaculture zones and centralized seed production zones. 4. Develop connection chains for producing, processing, consuming, ensuring stable outlets for more than 30% of aquaculture products. For the period of 2021 - 2025, the specific objectives include: 1. By 2030, total aquaculture productivity reaches 7,0 million tonne/year, export turnover reaches 12 billion USD/year, aquaculture value growth rate reaches 4,5 %/year on average. 2. Actively produce and supply more than 60% of giant tiger prawns parents and whiteleg shrimps parents, and 100% of selectively bred pangasius fish parents; improve breeders of aquaculture breeds with high economic value and great commodity quantity. 3. Invest, upgrade infrastructures essential for production of more than 50 centralized aquaculture zones and centralized seed production zones. 4. Develop connection chains for producing, processing, consuming, ensuring stable outlets for more than 50% of aquaculture products. The main tasks proposed in the document include: Improve aquaculture production capacity; Develop production of aquaculture breeds by species; Develop species-based rearing; Control quality, food safety, and traceability in aquaculture; Control epidemic and monitor the environment in aquaculture; Investment in upgrade of aquaculture infrastructures; Development of material and auxiliary industry production, supply systems for aquaculture; Improvement of human resource and organization; etc. In terms of developing species-based rearing, the  policy document proposes: Prioritize rearing methods for giant tiger prawns in mangrove forests, organic rearing methods, rice-shrimp rearing; continue to develop rearing methods for whiteleg shrimps in areas with suitable conditions to meet market demands, fulfill local planning for coastal provinces of the Mekong Delta; Closely control rearing facility conditions for pangasius fish in accordance with applicable laws, encourage expansion of rearing area in order to create high quality products satisfying food safety and market demands, and  encourage application of new, water-efficient rearing technologies adapting to climate change and wastewater and sludge treatment technologies; to take advantage of water surface potential of rivers, lakes, reservoirs, hydroelectricity reservoirs, irrigation reservoirs to develop aquaculture, supply food, create livelihood, promote poverty reduction and hunger eradication, and increase income of the general public; etc.

Title: Decision No. 1399/QD-BNN-TCCB defining functions, tasks, powers and organizational structure of the Department of Animal Health.
Keywords: institution; animal health; animal production; animal reproduction; pests/diseases; drugs; governance; institution; cattle; hygiene/sanitary procedures; meat; milk/dairy products; poultry products; sheep/goats; slaughtering; swine; food safety and quality; governance
Abstract: This Decision, consisting of 5 Articles, is issued pursuant to the Government’s Decree No. 15/2017/ND-CP defining functions, tasks, powers and organizational structure of Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development. The Department of Animal Health is an organization that is affiliated to the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development, and meant to assist the Minister of Agriculture and Rural Development in performing state management and organizing implementation of law on veterinary hygiene, safety of animal source foods and animal products.

Title: National Action Plan on Sustainable Consumption and Production (2021-2030).
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Environment; Food & nutrition; General
Keywords: sustainable use; sustainable development; certification; education; financing; good practices; innovation; internal trade; international cooperation; international trade; policy/planning; public procurement; research; sustainable development; certification; education; financing; good practices; innovation; internal trade; international cooperation; international trade; policy/planning; public procurement; research; ecofriendly products/ecofriendly processes; ecological production/organic production; subsidy/incentive; certification; education; financing; good practices; innovation; internal trade; international cooperation; international trade; policy/planning; public procurement; research; ecofriendly products/ecofriendly processes; ecological production/organic production; plastic; subsidy/incentive; waste management; sustainable development; certification; education; financing; good practices; innovation; internal trade; international cooperation; international trade; policy/planning; public procurement; research; ecofriendly products/ecofriendly processes; ecological production/organic production; plastic; recycling/reuse; subsidy/incentive
Abstract: General objectives of this National Action Plan on Sustainable Consumption and Production (2021-2030) are:  To promote efficient and sustainable management, exploitation and utilization of natural resources, fuel, materials; incentivize development of eco-friendly resources, fuel, materials, products which are renewable, reusable and recyclable; promote sustainable consumption and production on the basis of innovation, creativity, practice and development of sustainable consumption and production models; and promote sustainable consumption and production of domestic products, create stable and green jobs, enhance sustainable lifestyle and improve quality of people’s life towards a circular economy in Vietnam. Specific objectives by 2030 are set up in the Plan, which include: 1. Effectively improve and implement legal policies on sustainable consumption and production; 2. A 7 – 10% decrease in resources and materials used by major production sectors such as textile, steel, plastic, chemical, cement, alcohol and beer, beverage, paper, seafood processing and some other production sectors; 100% of industrial parks, clusters, trade villages organize communication to raise awareness on sustainable consumption and production; 3. Disseminate, replicate models of cleaner production, sustainable consumption and production; 4. 100% of provinces and cities directly under the Center organize communication, to raise awareness on sustainable lifestyles and consumption; 5. 100% of supermarkets, commercial centers use eco-friendly packaging which gradually replace single-use, non-degradable plastic items; etc. In order to achieve the objectives, main tasks are proposed in the Plan:  a) Improve legal policies on promoting sustainable consumption and production, including: develop and implement incentive policies for the green industry, environmental industry, waste recycling industry; formulate policies on promotion of investment, production, distribution, import, export of eco-friendly products, technologies; formulate policies on development of international trading and sustainable development in alignment with the roadmaps of international integration and commitment, performance of bilateral and multilateral trade agreements. b) Sustainably manage, exploit and use resources, develop renewable, recyclable materials, and fuel, including: Develop, apply and disseminate models of collection, reuse and recycling of waste, integrated management models of sustainable exploitation, processing and manufacturing, production and utilization of resources, materials, fuel and renewable energy for industrial sectors, etc. c) Sustainable design, ecological design, reuse-, recycle-oriented design. d) Promote cleaner production, production of eco-friendly products, apply sustainable linkage models following product life cycles. e) Develop system of sustainable distribution, sustainable import/export, including: Develop criteria, guidelines on certification of sustainable distributors, enterprises; certification and labeling of green commercial building; as well as provide information to identify green and sustainable distribution channels and systems; etc. f) Promote labeling and certification of ecolabels, including: establish, operate the system of ecological certification and labeling including green label, energy label, carbon label, recycling label, and other kinds of ecolabels, etc. g) Promote development of sustainable markets and provide information for consumers. h) Promote sustainable procurement. i) Build capacity, enhance education and implement sustainable lifestyles. j) Promote application of circular economy for waste. k) Develop science and technology to promote sustainable consumption and production. l) Promote access to and support of green finance. m) Promote international cooperation on sustainable consumption and production.

Title: Decision No. 02/QD-TTg 2022 approving the National Strategy on Nutrition for 2021-2030 and Vision to 2045.
Keywords: nutrition; policy/planning; research; capacity building; education; food fortification
Abstract: General objectives of this National Strategy on Nutrition for 2021-2030 and Vision to 2045 are: implement proper nutrition to improve the nutritional state suitable for each person, locality, region, and ethnicity, contributing to the decrease of disease and increase of stature, stamina, and intelligence of Vietnamese. Specific objectives by 2030 are set up in the Strategy, which include: a) Implementation of a varied, appropriate, and food-security diet for all ages and subjects according to the life cycle; b) Improvement of nutritional status for mothers, children, and teenagers; c) Control of overweight, prevention of non-infectious chronic diseases, related risk factors in children, teenagers, and adults; d) Reduction of micronutrient deficiency in children, teenagers, and women of childbearing age; e) Improvement of the nutritional reaction in emergency situations and enhancement of strategy implementation resource; etc. In order to achieve the objectives, main tasks are proposed in the Strategy: 1. Complete mechanisms and policies on nutrition; 2. Improve the inter-sectorial cooperation and social mobilization;  3. Strengthen communication and education on nutrition; 4. Strengthen and improve the quality of human resources; 5. Enhance technical expertise for the implementation of nutritional intervention; 6. Promote basic research and technology application research on nutrition and food suitable for Vietnamese; 7. Enhance technical development; research high technology application model serving nutrition purposes; etc.

Title: Decree No. 95/2022/ND-CP on defining functions, tasks, powers and organizational structure of Ministry of Health of Vietnam.
Keywords: public health; institution; governance; food safety and quality; institution; nutrition; governance
Abstract: This Decree defines the functions, tasks, powers and organizational structure of the Ministry of Health of Vietnam.The Ministry of Health of Vietnam is a governmental agency in charge of performing the state management of healthcare services, including the following fields: preventive medicine; medical examination and treatment, rehabilitation; medical assessment, forensic examination and forensic psychiatric assessment; traditional medicine; medical equipment; pharmaceutical products and cosmetics; food safety; health insurance; population, reproductive health; state management of public services rendered in the fields within the scope of its management. Regarding preventive medicine, the tasks and powers of the Ministry of Health include formulating and promulgating specialized regulations, national standards, national technical regulations, and economic - technical norms in the following fields: environmental health and hygiene, occupational health, school hygiene and health services; community nutrition; prevention and control of harmful effects of tobacco; prevention and control of harmful effects of alcoholic beverages; domestic water quality; etc. Regarding food safety, the tasks and powers of the Ministry of Health include: a) Play the leading role in formulating and promulgating national technical regulations on food products within the scope of its management; promulgate national technical regulations or regulations on safety limits of food product groups at the request of supervisory ministries; promulgate the list of food additives; b) Formulate and request competent authorities to promulgate regulations on food safety conditions to be satisfied by manufacturers and traders of functional foods, natural mineral water, bottled water, edible ice, micronutrients, food additives, food flavorings, food processing aids, devices and materials for directly packaging or storing foods; industrial kitchens, restaurants of hotels, resorts, other restaurants, food and beverage trading establishments and street food vendors; c) Play the leading role in disseminating information and knowledge and educate about laws on food safety within the scope of its management; d) Play the leading role and cooperate with relevant ministries and regulatory authorities in building an alert system on food safety incidents; providing specific provisions on statement of food safety incidents; organizing the supervision, analysis of food safety risks, prevention, investigation and cooperation in preventing food poisoning as well as responding to food safety risks within the scope of its management; etc.

Title: Circular No. 15/2022/TT-BNNPTNT on inspection and supervision of veterinary hygiene and food safety for honey.
Keywords: apiculture/sericulture; animal health; inspection; international trade; internal trade; processing/handling; animal production; drugs; inspection; international trade; internal trade; processing/handling; food safety and quality; hygiene/sanitary procedures; apiculture/sericulture
Abstract: This Circular No. 15/2022/TT-BNNPTNT prescribes inspection and supervision of veterinary hygiene and food safety in beekeeping, purchase, preparation, and processing of honey for domestic consumption and export. The Department of Animal Health shall inspect and supervise honey production facilities (which include beekeeping facilities, honey purchasing facilities, and honey processing facilities) for export; honey production facilities for both export and domestic consumption. Article 8 sets up the basis for developing supervision program. Article 9 provides for the detail rules of supervision program.

Title: Joint Circular No. 13/2016/TTLT-BYT-BGDDT regulations on healthcare activities in schools.
Keywords: school feeding; food safety and quality; public health; potable water; public health
Abstract: This Joint Circular stipulates regulations on healthcare activities in schools in respects of facilities, equipment, environment and other healthcare services for students. Article 5 provides for the requirements for water supply and drainage and environmental hygiene in schools. Every school shall supply sufficient drinking water in a quantity of at least 0.5 liter per student/class in summer and 0.3 liter per student/class in winter. Article 6 provides for the requirements for food safety. For schools with canteens for boarding students or semi-boarding students, every canteen in schools shall meet requirements stipulated in Article 4 of the Circular No.30/2012/TT-BYT on food safety and hygiene for food and beverage services and street vendors dated December 05, 2012 by the Minister of Health.

Title: Circular No. 30/2012/TT-BYT promulgating the regulations on food safety conditions for food services business and street food business establishments..
Keywords: food safety and quality; processing/handling; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection; vending; internal trade
Abstract: This Circular specifies the conditions for food services business and street food business establishments. The Circular applies to organizations or individuals, households operating food services business and street food business in the territory of Vietnam. Article 3 provides for condotions for ready-made helping processing establishment. Article 4 provides for condotions for canteen, collective kitchen, kitchen, hotel's restaurant, resort restaurant. Remaining articles make provisions, inter alia, for:  conditions for food safety assurance for street food business, inspecting food services business and street food business establishment, etc.

Title: Circular No. 23/2019/TT-BCT on regulating non-application of tariff quotas for importation of sugar of ASEAN member states' origin.
Keywords: international trade; sugar
Abstract: This Circular promulgated by the Minister of Industry and Trade regulates the non-application of tariff quotas for importation of sugar of the ASEAN member states' origin. Import tariff quotas shall not be applied to sugar commodities of the ASEAN member states’ origin in accordance with laws in force. The quantities of sugar imported from ASEAN states shall not be counted as the annual in-quota quantities announced by the Ministry of Industry and Trade according to the WTO schedule of commitments applicable to its member states.

Title: Decree No. 15/2018/ND-CP of the Government on elaboration of some articles of the Law on Food safety.
Keywords: food safety and quality; international trade; internal trade; certification; registration; consumer protection; genetically modified organism (GMO); processing/handling; hygiene/sanitary procedures; inspection; packaging/labelling; vending; institution; traceability; governance
Abstract: This Decree elaborates some Articles of the Law of Food safety on: 1. Procedures for product self-declaration. 2. Procedures for registration of the product declaration. 3. Assurance of genetically modified GM foods. 4. Issuance of the certificate of food safety. 5. State inspection of safety of imported and exported foods. 6. Food labeling. 7. Food advertisements. 8. Food safety requirements in production of dietary supplements. 9. Food safety requirements in production, sale and use of food additives. 10. Tracing food origins. 11. Food safety authorities. Every food manufacturer and seller must obtain the certificate of food safety, except for those specified in Clause 1 Article 12 of this Decree. Requirements for issuance of the certificate of food safety are specified in Clause 1 Article 34 of the Law of Food safety. Manufacturers of dietary supplements shall apply the requirements specified in Article 28 of this Decree. Manufacturers and sellers of foods the content of GM ingredients in which exceeds 5% of total ingredients, in addition to compliance with common regulations of law on goods labeling, the goods label must contain information about the GMOs, except for the cases specified in Clause 2 of Article 10 of this Decree.

Title: Circular No. 15/2012/TT-BYT  Providing on General Conditions to Ensure Food Safety for Food Production and Business Facilities.
Keywords: food safety and quality; processing/handling; hygiene/sanitary procedures; internal trade
Abstract: This Circular is siisued in accordance with the Law of Food safety, and the Government’s Decree No.38/2012/ND-CP detailing a number of articles of the Law of Food safety. Chapter I and II provide for the general conditions to ensure food safety for food production facilities  and food business facilities, including: requirements for facility, requirements for equipments, tools, requirements for person directly performing food business, etc.

Title: Circular No. 04/2023/TT-BCT on prescribing application of import tariff-rate quotas for unmanufactured tobacco and poultry eggs originated from member states of Eurasian Economic Union in 2023 - 2027 period.
Keywords: international trade; allocation/quota; poultry products; international trade; tobacco; agricultural commodities
Abstract: This Circular prescribes the application of import tariff-rate quotas for unmanufactured tobacco and poultry eggs originated from member states of Eurasian Economic Union in 2023 - 2027 period. The import tariff-rate quotas for unmanufactured tobacco shall be allocated to traders that hold valid license for manufacturing of cigarettes issued by the Ministry of Industry and Trade of Vietnam and wish to import unmanufactured tobacco to serve their manufacturing of cigarettes. The import tariff-rate quotas for poultry eggs shall be allocated to traders that wish to import poultry eggs.

Title: Circular 28/2019/TT-BNNPTNT of the Minister of Agriculture and Agricultural Development  Regulating the Technical Requirements for Testing Prohibited Chemicals and Antibiotics in Aquatic Foods.
Keywords: aquatic animals; aquatic plants; fish products; drugs; fish diseases; animal health; antimicrobial resistance (AMR); aquatic animals; aquatic plants; fish products; food safety and quality; MRL-maximum residue limit; residues; drugs
Abstract: This Circular provides technical requirements for testing for some indicators of chemicals and antibiotics banned from being used in fishery foods with prescribed minimum required performance levels (MRPL) under the management of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development. MRPLs for some chemicals and antibiotics banned from being used in fishery foods are provided for in the Appendix. The CCβ value for a chemical or antibiotic banned from being used in fishery foods must be less than or equal to (≤) the corresponding MRPL prescribed in the Appendix. Each testing laboratory is required to be assessed and designated by a competent authority to perform testing for some chemicals and antibiotics banned from being used in fishery foods with established permitted MRPLs in order to serve the performance of food safety management tasks by the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development as prescribed herein. Exported fishery foods shall conform to domestic regulations of importing countries and not be governed by this Circular.

Title: Decision No. 150/QD-TTg on approving the sustainable agriculture and rural development strategies for the period 2021 – 2030 with a vision toward 2050.
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Livestock; Food & nutrition; Fisheries; Forestry; Cultivated plants
Keywords: agricultural development; rural development; sustainable development; gender; equity; rural employment; smallholders/peasants; poverty; climate change; agricultural commodities; cooperative/producer organization; innovation; labour; agricultural development; animal production; sustainable development; fish products; fishery management and conservation; sustainable development; inland fisheries; marine fisheries; aquaculture; internal trade; international trade; food safety and quality; food security; afforestation/reforestation; biodiversity; ecosystem conservation; equity; gender; sustainable development; sustainable use; timber; climate change; forest management/forest conservation; agricultural development; rural development; sustainable development; gender; equity; rural employment; smallholders/peasants; climate change; agricultural commodities; cooperative/producer organization; labour
Abstract: This policy document is a sectoral policy document at the national level. Vision toward 2050 is set up as: Vietnam is expected to become one of the leading agricultural countries of the world with a modern, efficient and environmentally friendly agricultural product processing industry. Rural areas will no longer have poor households and will become a worth-living, civilized, green, clean and beautiful place with rural residents’ living conditions and incomes comparable with those of the urban area. Specific objectives until 2030 have been identified, which include: 1. Growth of GDP from agriculture, forestry and fishing is expected to reach an average of 2,5% - 3%/year, growth of labor productivity in agriculture, forestry and fishing is expected to reach an average of 5,5 - 6%/year.  2. Expand and develop markets, especially export markets. Growth of the export value of agricultural, forested and aquatic products is expected to reach an average of 5 - 6%/year. 3. Increase people’s income; achieve sustainable poverty reduction. Rural residents’ income is expected to be 2.5 - 3 times higher than in 2020. Percentage of multidimensional poverty households in rural areas is expected to decrease on average by 1-1.5%/year. 4. Proportion of agricultural workers in the total social workers is expected to decrease to less than 20%, percentage of trained agricultural workers is expected to reach over 70%. 5. In the whole country, at least 90% of communes will meet the new rural standards, of which 50% of the communes will meet the newly enhanced rural standards. 6. Develop environmentally friendly and green agriculture; adapt to climate change, reduce rural environmental pollution, strive to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 10% compared to 2020. The forest cover rate is expected to remain sustainably at 42%, the forest area with a certificate of sustainable forest management is expected to reach over 1 million hectares. Strategic directions and tasks have been proposed in the policy document, including: 1. Completing the structure of agricultural production in association with competitive advantages and market requirements; 2.Organizing important stages in production to improve efficiency and ensure sustainable development; 3.Promoting cooperation and association to develop value chains and advanced agricultural models; 4. Developing rural economy to create jobs and increase incomes for rural populations; 5. Developing civilized and modern rural areas associated with urbanization, preserving traditional culture; 6. Inclusive development, assurance of equity of social welfare in rural areas through developing equal social welfare policies (house and settlement, health insurance, retirement regime, etc.) for rural residents and aiming for gender equality; 7. Developing a strong community to act as the key for rural development and agriculture production; 8. Protecting the ecological environment and landscapes in order to adapt to climate change. Main measures to be taken include: 1. Carry out dissemination and education to innovate thinking and unify perception and actions; 2. Innovate production and business organizations, complete production relationship; 3. Improve the quality of vocational training; qualifications, efficiency of research, application and transfer of science, technology and innovation; 4. Develop domestic and foreign markets in order to ensure sustainable outputs for agricultural products; 5. Develop and complete infrastructure; 6. Carry out innovations and improve the capacity and effectiveness of state management; 7. Promote digital transformation in rural agriculture sectors; 8. Proactively adapt to climate change, manage risks.

Title: Resolution on the National Land Use Plan for the period 2021 - 2030, with a vision to 2050 and the 5-year National Land Use Plan 2021 - 2025.
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Food & nutrition; Land & soil
Keywords: policy/planning; agricultural development; agricultural land; flood; protection of environment; soil conservation/soil improvement; soil pollution/quality; sustainable development; sustainable use; irrigation; financial agricultural measures; climate change; hazards; pollution control; food security; policy/planning; policy/planning; agricultural development; agricultural land; flood; protection of environment; soil conservation/soil improvement; soil pollution/quality; sustainable development; sustainable use; land-use planning; climate change; data collection/reporting; hazards; pollution control
Abstract: The Goals of the National Land Use Plan for the period 2021 - 2030, with a vision to 2050 and the 5-year National Land Use Plan 2021 - 2025 include: a) Ensuring the demand for land use for the implementation of the 10-year socio-economic development strategy 2021-2030, the 5-year socio-economic development plan 2021-2025. Reasonable and economical allocation, efficient land resources for sectors, fields and localities in socio-economic development, defense and security assurance, associated with environmental protection, adaptation to climate change; creating a foundation for our country to become a developed, high-income country by 2045; b) Arranging land fund to meet the requirements of developing a synchronous infrastructure system, ensuring the connection of inter-sectoral and inter-regional development space, economic corridors and development driving regions of the country; stabilize 3.5 million hectares of rice land; ensure a stable forest cover rate at 42 - 43%; c) Reclaim, restore, encroach on the sea, put unused land into use; limit land degradation; renovate and restore degraded land areas in association with environmental protection and sustainable development. To achieve the Goals, tasks have been proposed in the policy document, including: The land use space shall be allocated based on the potential of agro-ecological zones, advantages of coastal economic corridors and 6 socio-economic zones, ensuring the balance of food security requirements, millennium target on environment, culture, national defense and security; ensure the balance of the natural ecosystem; Develop areas of high-quality rice, high-tech agriculture, and clean agriculture in the Red River Delta region; to step up the agricultural product processing industry and the consumer goods production industry in the southern region of the region; Accelerate digital transformation, strive to complete and connect the land information system and national land database by 2025, including data on management planning manage and update changes to each land parcel, publicly and transparently, creating conditions for people and businesses to access information and data on land use master plans and plans; Ensure resources, especially financial resources, to perfect the land information system and the national land database in a centralized and unified manner; ensure resources to fulfill the targets in the land use planning; To rationally and effectively exploit unused land and alluvial land along rivers and along the coast for forest development and green tree development in urban centers and industrial parks; strictly manage natural forests, special-use forests and protection forests; Promote and encourage the development of urban centers, industrial parks, and export processing zones according to the circular model with low greenhouse gas emissions, ensuring land fund for the development of inter-regional waste treatment and recycling zones; Select advanced and low-emission technologies in attracting projects using land with environmentally sensitive areas; Relocating places, residential areas, production facilities out of areas and areas at risk of natural disasters, geological hazards, landslides and subsidence; Strengthening solutions to improve and protect the environment of land, water and biodiversity, especially for degraded agricultural production land; prevent and minimize land degradation and soil pollution; etc.

Title: Circular No. 25/2018/TT-BNNPTNT of the Minister of Agriculture and Rural Development stipulating the order and procedures for risk assessment , license to import live aquatic animals and plants.
Keywords: aquatic animals; aquatic plants; fish products; international trade; authorization/permit; risk assessment/management; institution; governance; aquatic animals; aquatic plants; fish products; international trade; authorization/permit; risk assessment/management; institution; governance
Abstract: This Circular provides guidelines for regulations in Clause 4 and 6 Article 98 of the Law on Fisheries regarding procedures for risk assessment of and license for import of live aquatic animals and plants not on the list of aquatic species permitted to be traded in Vietnam for food, decoration, entertainment and display in fairs, exhibitions and scientific research. This Circular applies to organizations and individuals related to management, import, transport, nurture, processing and use of imported live aquatic animals and plants not on the list of aquatic species permitted to be traded in Vietnam for food, decoration, entertainment and display in fairs, exhibitions and scientific research. A license for import of live aquatic animals and plants in case of risk assessment shall be granted if such live aquatic animals and plants are imported for food, decoration and entertainment purpose for the first time. A license for import of live aquatic animals and plants without risk assessment shall be granted in case: a) live animals and plants are imported for food, decoration and entertainment that have undergone risk assessment; b) live aquatic animals and plants are imported for scientific research; c) live aquatic animals and plants are imported for display in fairs and exhibitions. Article 8-9 provide for the contents and methods of risk assessment of imported live aquatic animals and plants. A risk assessment council shall be established (art.10).

Title: Circular No. 11/2021/TT-BNNPTNT of the Minister of Agriculture and Rural Development promulgating the HS code table for the list of goods under the state management authority of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development and the list of export and import goods subject to specialized inspection in the field of agriculture and rural development.
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Livestock; Food & nutrition; Fisheries; Forestry; Cultivated plants
Keywords: international trade; agricultural commodities; pests/diseases; inspection; pesticides; international trade; animal health; quarantine; inspection; drugs; aquaculture; animal feed/feedstuffs; international trade; condiments/herbs/spices; international trade; timber; plant protection; inspection; pesticides; fertilizers/nutrients; international trade; agricultural commodities; quarantine; plant protection; inspection; pesticides; fertilizers/nutrients; plant variety; plant production
Abstract: This Circular is issued by the Minister of Agriculture and Rural Development,  promulgating HS codes of  commodities under management of Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development and imports and exports  subject to specialized inspection in the field of agriculture and rural development. Annex I contains Lists of HS codes of commodities under management of Ministry of Agriculture and Rural  Development, including: Section 1. List of HS codes of terrestrial animals and products thereof subject to quarantine; Section 2. List of HS codes of other animals in the field of animal husbandry; Section 3. List of HS codes of livestock breeds banned from export; etc. Annex II provide for the List of HS codes of imports subject to specialized inspection before grant of customs clearance under  the management of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development. Annex III gives the List of HS codes of imports subject to specialized inspection after grant of customs clearance under  the management of the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development.

Title: Circular No. 29/2023/TT-BYT on contents and presentation of nutrition facts on food labels.
Keywords: food safety and quality; nutrition; packaging/labelling; internal trade; international trade
Abstract: This Circular provides guidance on contents and presentation of nutrients and their values (nutrition facts) on food labels, and the roadmap for implementation with regard to prepackaged foods that are manufactured, sold, imported into, circulated in Vietnam. Nutrition facts on food labels shall be written in accordance with regulations of this Circular, relevant regulations of law on goods labels and food safety. Nutrition facts must be accurate and do not cause confusion or misunderstanding of nutritional value of the food. Nutrition facts on food labels must be recognizable, comprehensible and indelible. The following nutrient contents shall be specified on labels of foods that are manufactured, sold, imported into, circulated in Vietnam: a) Energy; b) Protein; c) Carbohydrate; d) Fat; e) Sodium.

Title: Decree No. 108/2011/ND-CP  providing amendments to the Government’s Decree No. 69/2010/ND-CP prescribing biosafety of genetically modified organisms, genetic specimens and products of genetically modified organisms.
Keywords: genetically modified organism (GMO); research; transport/storage; certification; international trade; biosafety; biodiversity; genetically modified organism (GMO); research; transport/storage; certification; international trade; biosafety; biotechnology; genetically modified organism (GMO); research; transport/storage; certification; international trade; biosafety; biotechnology
Abstract: This Decree amends a number of articles of the Government's Decree No. 69/2010/ND-CP of June 21, 2010, on biosafety for genetically modified organisms, genetic specimens and products of genetically modified organisms.

Title: Decree No. 118/2020/ND-CP on amendments to Decree No. 69/2010/ND-CP  of the Government on biosafety for genetically modified organisms, genetic specimens and products of genetically modified organisms.
Keywords: genetically modified organism (GMO); research; transport/storage; certification; international trade; biosafety; biodiversity; authorization/permit; genetically modified organism (GMO); research; transport/storage; packaging/labelling; certification; international trade; biosafety; biotechnology; authorization/permit; genetically modified organism (GMO); research; transport/storage; packaging/labelling; certification; international trade; biosafety; biotechnology; authorization/permit
Abstract: This Decree amends a number of articles of the Government's Decree No. 69/2010/ND-CP of June 21, 2010, on biosafety for genetically modified organisms, genetic specimens and products of genetically modified organisms. Article 19 is amended  as follows: “Article 19. Permit for testing genetically modified organisms Permit for testing genetically modified organisms shall follow Form No. 6 under Annex attached to this Decree;”  Article 19a. Entitlement and procedures for reissuance of permit for testing genetically modified organisms is added Article 19a after Article 19. Other amendments are made on Article 3, 5, 7,15, 16, 17, 18, 23, 28, etc.

Title: Circular on Traceability of Food-related Products under Management of Ministry of Health.
Keywords: food safety and quality; traceability; Codex Alimentarius; consumer protection; data collection/reporting; food additives; processing/handling; internal trade; transport/storage
Abstract: This Circular prescribes principles and cases of traceability and responsibility to implement traceability with respect to food, micronutrients in food, additives, aromatic spices, processing aids, tools, packaging and container materials that come food processing aids that come into direct contact with food under management of Ministry of Health  specified under Annex II attached to Decree No. 15/2018/ND-CP dated February 2, 2018 of Government. The Circular applies to entities involved in the manufacturing and distribution of food products under the Ministry's management. The traceability process follows a one step forward , one step back approach, allowing for the identification of the previous and next manufacturing processes in the supply chain. Key requirements outlined in the circular include the establishment of traceability databases by food related product manufacturing and business establishments. These databases must contain detailed information on product batches, origins, safety measures, ingredients, and packaging materials. Additionally, entities are responsible for maintaining these records for a specified period and promptly tracing potentially unsafe products upon detection or request by authorities. The Circular emphasizes the importance of reporting implementation results to competent authorities and taking necessary actions in case of unsafe products, including recalls and legal compliance. It also mandates the application of traceability databases based on product codes as requested by authorities.

Title: Resolution on Ensuring National Food Security by 2030 (No: 34/NQ-CP).
Domain: Agricultural & rural dev.; Livestock; Food & nutrition; Fisheries; Cultivated plants
Keywords: food security; internal trade; international cooperation; international trade; investment; agricultural commodities; climate change; capacity building; hazards; food security; internal trade; international cooperation; international trade; investment; agricultural commodities; animal production; climate change; capacity building; hazards; food security; internal trade; international cooperation; international trade; investment; fish products; climate change; capacity building; hazards; food security; nutrition; cereals/grains/rice; innovation; internal trade; international cooperation; international trade; investment; meat; milk/dairy products; agricultural commodities; climate change; capacity building; hazards; food security; internal trade; international cooperation; international trade; investment; agricultural commodities; climate change; capacity building; hazards
Abstract: The overall goals of this policy document are: ensure sufficient food for domestic consumption in all situations and partly for export; increase people's income to ensure access to quality and safe food; gradually improve the stature, physical strength and intelligence of Vietnamese people. The specific objectives by 2030 are set up, including: Flexible use of rice land fund, improving the efficiency of rice land use, maintaining 3.5 million hectares of rice land, producing at least 35 million tons of rice annually, serving as the core to ensure national food security, meeting the needs of consumption, processing, storage and export;  Farmers producing rice in large-scale concentrated rice production areas have an average profit of over 35% compared to production costs. Rural people's income is 2 times higher than in 2020. Synchronously develop infrastructure , circulation and distribution systems to increase people's access to food; Ensure nutritional needs with a balanced diet, reduce the proportion of rice used, increase the use of meat, milk, eggs, fish, vegetables, and fruits of all kinds, contributing to improving the quality of life for people, especially school-age children. To achieve the objectives, the main tasks and solutions are proposed, including: 1. Promote development and restructuring of food production, linked to the market; 2. Investing in developing infrastructure to serve food production; 3. Strengthen research, application and transfer of science and technology in food production, preservation and processing; 4. Training to improve the quality of human resources; 5. Innovation in food production organization forms; 6. Innovation of policy mechanisms to ensure national food security; 7. Develop the circulation system, increase access to food for people anytime, anywhere; 8. Developing information and communication systems on national food security; 9. Improve the efficiency of resource use, protect the environment, improve the ability to adapt to climate change, and prevent natural disasters; 10. Promote international cooperation in the field of food security.

Title: Decision 1294/QD-BYT 2022 issuing  the Action Plan to implement the National Nutrition Strategy to 2025.
Keywords: nutrition; policy/planning; capacity building; education; investment; monitoring; hazards; insurance; public health; gender
Abstract: This Action Plan to Implement the National Nutrition Strategy to 2025 outlines the country's commitment to improving nutrition through various programs and actions, aiming to enhance public health and nutrition from 2021 to 2025. The Action Plan identifies several challenges, including lack of investment in nutrition by local governments, insufficient financial resources for nutrition programs, poor inter-sectoral coordination, outdated policies, and limited capacity of the nutrition workforce. It takes into account the changing nutritional landscape, including the rise in overweight, obesity, and micronutrient deficiencies alongside traditional malnutrition. It sets specific goals and targets for 2025, focusing on diverse and balanced diets, improving nutrition for mothers, children, and adolescents, controlling overweight and obesity, and enhancing micronutrient status.  Key initiatives include strengthening social media outreach to disseminate nutritional education through platforms like TikTok, Instagram, and Zalo. The establishment of a national electronic portal for nutrition and food management is also highlighted, alongside the development of educational materials tailored to different age groups and specific audiences, such as parents and teachers. The Action Plan stresses the need for training and capacity building for nutrition professionals to improve the quality of nutrition services across the country. It also addresses emergency nutrition responses, particularly in situations like natural disasters and disease outbreaks, ensuring that local models are adapted to meet community needs. Furthermore, the Action Plan outlines efforts to enhance healthcare services in rural areas, improve the nutritional status of women and children, and increase health insurance coverage among the population. Potential sources of funding has been identified,  including national budgets, community mobilization, international support. The Annexes details specific programs, projects, and monitoring indicators related to nutrition.

Title: Decision 718/QD-BYT 2018 approving the National Action Plan on Nutrition by 2020.
Keywords: nutrition; policy/planning; capacity building; education; investment; monitoring; hazards; public health; financing
Abstract: This National Action Plan on Nutrition outlines Vietnam's strategic approach to improving the nutritional status of its population by 2020, in alignment with the national nutrition strategy for the 2011-2020 period with a vision towards 2030. The Action Plan is a collaborative effort involving ministries, local governments, and citizens, highlighting the need for interdisciplinary cooperation and investment in nutrition, especially in disadvantaged areas. It emphasizes the importance of balanced nutrition for the comprehensive development of citizens, particularly in enhancing height, strength, and intellect. Key objectives include reducing stunting among children under five to less than 21.5% in the northern highlands and less than 28% in the central highlands, reducing wasting to below 5%, and addressing chronic energy deficiency among women of reproductive age. The plan also focuses on ensuring proper nutrition during the critical first 1,000 days of life, from conception to two years of age, to prevent stunting effectively. To achieve these goals, the plan includes various strategies such as improving the quality of meals, promoting food security, and enhancing physical activity among students. It also calls for the development of guidelines for nutrition therapy and clinical nutrition, as well as the establishment of monitoring systems to assess progress. Human resource development is crucial, with a focus on training nutrition officials and improving their capacity at all levels. The plan encourages the involvement of social organizations and the private sector in nutrition operations. Funding for these initiatives will come from various sources, including the state budget, particularly for vulnerable populations.
